items.description: The VA CART SYNTAX risk model predicts 30-day post-Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI) mortality, using a combination of clinical and anatomic variables. The model is available to facilitate personalized informed consent discussions and appropriate preparation for high-risk PCI cases.

items.name: VA CART Mortality Model
items.description: The VA CART mortality risk model is based on logistic regression using baseline clinical and procedural variables to predict post-PCI 30-day mortality. The model is available to facilitate risk stratification at the point of care.

items.name: VA CART Nephropathy Risk Model
items.description: The VA CART nephropathy risk model is based on logistic regression using baseline clinical and procedural variables to predict Post-Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI) acute kidney injury (AKI). The model is available to facilitate risk stratification at the point of care. In operations and maintenance.

items.name: Brainlab Elements Image Fusion, Contouring (4.5);Image Fusion (4.5);Fibertracking (2.0);BOLD MRI Mapping (1.0);Image Fusion Angio (1.0)
items.description: Brainlab Elements are software applications indicated for the processing of medical image data to support the intended user group to perform image guided surgery and radiation treatment planning. The Brainlab Elements 6.0 are applications that transfer Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine (DICOM) data to and from picture archiving and communication systems (PACS) and other storage media devices. They include modules for 2D & 3D image viewing, image processing, image co-registration, image segmentation and a 3D visualization of medical image data for treatment planning procedures.

items.name: BioPlex 2200 ANA Screen with Medical Decision Support Software for Use with BioPlex 2200 Multi-Analyte Detection System
items.description: The BioPlex 2200 Medical Decision Support Software (MDSS) is a pattern recognition algorithm that can enhance the performance of the Antinuclear Antibody (ANA) Screening associated diagnostic patterns, among its multiple assay results.

items.name: VA CART Bleeding Risk Model
items.description: The VA CART bleeding risk model is based on logistic regression, using baseline clinical and procedural variables to predict post-Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI) in-hospital bleeding events. The model is available to facilitate risk stratification at the point of care. It is in operations and maintenance.

items.name: Biograph Vision, Biograph MCT Family Of PET/CTs
items.description: The Biograph Vision and Biograph mCT systems provide registration and fusion of high-resolution metabolic and anatomic information from the two major components of each system (Positron emission tomography (PET) and computed tomography (CT)). These systems are designed for whole body oncology, neurology and cardiology examinations.

items.name: AutoContour Model RADAC V3
items.description: AutoContour is intended to assist radiation treatment planners in contouring and reviewing structures within medical images in preparation for radiation therapy treatment planning.

items.name: Aplio i900, Aplio i800 and Aplio i700 Software V8.1 Diagnostic Ultrasound System
items.description: The Diagnostic Ultrasound System Aplio i900 Model TUS-AI900, Aplio i800 Model TUS-AI800, Aplio i700 Model TUS-AI700 are indicated for the visualization of structures and dynamic processes within the human body, using ultrasound, and to provide image information for diagnosis in the following clinical applications: fetal, abdominal, intra-operative (abdominal), pediatric, small organs (thyroid, breast and testicle), trans-vaginal, trans-rectal, neonatal cephalic, adult cephalic, cardiac (both adult and pediatric), peripheral vascular, transesophageal, musculo-skeletal (both conventional and superficial), laparoscopic and Thoracic/Pleural. This system provides high-quality ultrasound images in the following modes: B mode, M mode, Continuous Wave, Color Doppler, Pulsed Wave Doppler , Power Doppler and Combination Doppler, as well as Speckle-tracking, Tissue Harmonic Imaging, Combined Modes, Shear wave, Elastography, and Acoustic attenuation mapping. This system is suitable for use in hospital and clinical settings by physicians or legally qualified persons who have received the appropriate training. In addition to the aforementioned indications for use, when the Endoscopic Ultrasound (EUS) transducer GF-UCT180 is connected, Aplio i800 Model TUS-AI800/E3 provides image information for the diagnosis of the upper gastrointestinal tract and surrounding organs.

items.name: Alignment System Cranial, Alignment Software Cranial, Cirq Alignment Software Cranial Biopsy, Cirq Alignment Software Cranial sEEG, Varioguide Alignment Software Cranial
items.description: Alignment System Cranial is intended to support the surgeon in planning and achieving a trajectory with surgical instruments during cranial stereotactic procedures. The indications for use are biopsy of intracranial lesions and placement of stereoelectroencephalography (SEEG) electrodes.

items.name: Acumen Hypotension Prediction Index - EV1000 Clinical Platform, Acumen Hypotension Prediction Index - Hemosphere Advanced Monitoring Platform, Acumen Hypotension Prediction Index, Hemosphere Advanced Monitoring Platform - Pressure
items.description: The Edwards Lifesciences Acumen Hypotension Prediction Index feature provides the clinician with physiological insight into a patient’s likelihood of future hypotensive events (defined as mean arterial pressure < 65 mmHg for at least one minute in duration) and the associated hemodynamics. The Acumen HPI feature is intended for use in surgical or non-surgical patients receiving advanced hemodynamic monitoring. The Acumen HPI feature is considered to be additional quantitative information regarding the patient’s physiological condition for reference only and no therapeutic decisions should be made based solely on the Hypotension Prediction Index (HPI) parameter.

items.name: Continuous Glucose Monitoring Summary
items.description: Reduce work hours associated with manually scouring Ambulatory Glucose Profile metrics and reduce transcription errors while copying and pasta data between medical tools and platforms.

items.name: CarestreamCS 9600
items.description: CS 9600 is an extraoral system intended to produce two-dimensional and three-dimensional digital X-ray images of the dento-maxilofacial, ENT (Ear, Nose and Throat), cervical spine and wrist regions at the direction of healthcare professionals, as diagnostic support for pediatric and adult patients.

items.name: No Missed Malignancy (NMM)
items.description: Enhance clinical decision support capabilities and provide information not easily obtained through existing systems so that abnormal radiology findings are acted upon promptly—reducing risk of delayed cancer diagnoses and aligning with federal standards for patient safety and clinical accountability. The model has been successfully deployed to production.

items.name: Sleep Apnea
items.description:
Improve Veteran care by increasing access to diagnosis and treatment of Veterans at high risk for sleep apnea, a condition that is not recognized in an estimated 80-90% of people who have it but poses significant negative impact on quality of life, productivity, sleep satisfaction, daytime fatigue, motor vehicle accidents, hypertension, and possibly other comorbidities(associations via observational trials of sleep apnea and cardiovascular morbidity and mortality)

The AI solution predicts a patient's risk of Sleep Apnea. Clinicians use this information to increase access to diagnosis and treatment of patients at high risk for sleep apnea.  A data pipeline, not a person, gathers the data and a LLM summarizes the information.  Outputs are graded by humans to determine accuracy.

items.name: Coordinated Care Tracking System (CCTS) Generative AI Support
items.description: To support the CCTS application we have created a generative AI pipeline that goes through all of the radiology and pathology notes for a day and extracts all references to all cancer related findings (tumors, nodules, masses, etc) and extracts all of the characteristics and comments of the findings.  These extracts will then be integrated in the CCTS application to ensure that proper diagnostic codes have been added to the scans so that a dangerous tumor is not missed.  There is no fine-tuning with this system.  Extracts from the notes are graded by humans to assess accuracy. This will provide insights to inform case follow up, scalable computing power for the CCTS clinical application​.

items.name: Siemens Sequoia Crown
items.description: The ACUSON Sequoia and Sequoia Select ultrasound imaging systems are intended to provide images of, or signals from, inside the body by an appropriately trained healthcare professional in a clinical setting for the following applications: Fetal, Abdominal, Pediatric, Neonatal Cephalic, Small Parts ,Obstetrics & Gynecology (OB/GYN) (useful for visualization of the ovaries, follicles, uterus and other pelvic structures), Cardiac, Pelvic, Vascular, Adult Cephalic, Musculoskeletal and Peripheral Vascular applications.

items.name: Philips 7300 C
items.description: AI-assisted Smart Collimation Thorax algorithm, developed by Philips, adjusts detector height and proposes collimation for each chest upright patient individually, based upon data from a 3D Camera.

items.name: ARANZ Medical Silhouette Wound Management System
items.description: Evaluate parameter, depth, and area of a wound (image): The ARANZ Medical Silhouette system is employed in the VA for monitoring and tracking wounds.

items.name: VASQIP Mortality Calculator
items.description:
Tracking Surgical Outcomes: Collecting data on surgical procedures and outcomes, such as complications, morbidity, and mortality rates.

Benchmarking Performance: Comparing surgical outcomes at VA facilities against national standards and best practices to identify areas where performance can be improved.

Quality Improvement Initiatives: Implementing targeted interventions and quality improvement projects based on the analysis of collected data to enhance patient care.

Reporting and Accountability: Providing regular reports on surgical outcomes and quality metrics to promote transparency and accountability within the VA healthcare system.

items.name: Frailty Risk Analysis
items.description: Define frailty in surgical patients: screen for potential frailty in pre-surgical patients

items.name: Butterfly iQ3 Ultrasound System
items.description: The Butterfly iQ3 Ultrasound System is indicated for use by qualified and trained healthcare professionals to enable diagnostic ultrasound imaging and measurement of anatomical structures and fluids of adult and pediatric patients for the following clinical applications: Peripheral Vessel (including carotid, deep vein thrombosis and arterial studies), Small Organs (including thyroid, scrotum and breast), Cardiac, Abdominal, Lung, Procedural Guidance, Urology, Fetal/Obstetric, Gynecological, Musculoskeletal (conventional), Musculoskeletal (superficial) and Ophthalmic.

items.name: CareView Patient Safety System
items.description: It monitors patient movement to watch out for fall risks.

items.name: GE Omni Legend
items.description: The Omni Legend positron emission tomography (PET)/computed tomography scan (CT) system is intended for CT attenuation corrected, anatomically localized PET imaging of the distribution of positron-emitting radiopharmaceuticals. It is intended to develop an image of the whole body, head, heart, brain, lung, breast, bone, gastrointestinal and lymphatic systems, and other organs. The system is also intended for stand-alone, diagnostic CT imaging.

items.name: Volpara Imaging Software
items.description: Generates volumetric measurements of breast x-ray scans.

items.name: UiPath Document Understanding
items.description: This tool has the ability to intelligentially pull data from different types of documents and transfer that to other applications so that a human does not have to.  It makes this type of input be more accurate and can be run 24/7/365 if needed leaving the staff to help with forms/documents that cannot be interpreted by the automated system.

items.name: Axon Body Camera and DMS - OSSO
items.description: AI is used to aid in Redaction of recorded Video footage and recorded Audio footage. Additionally, AI is used via transcribing recorded videos.

items.name: GE Logiq E10
items.description: Accelerate ultrasound workflow with AI assistant tools.

items.name: ReflexAI
items.description: Crisis Responders gain more practice.

items.name: App feedback model for NLP tasks
items.description:
The objective is to utilize Natural Language Processing (NLP) with comment reviews for Appfeedback, specifically to identify named entities (NER), profanity, and stop words, and provide an automated approach to pre-processing and cleaning text for downstream analytics tools.
The system uses spaCy's en_core_web_sm model, an open-source software and model, to analyze text reviews from various sources, including external sources (Google and Apple stores) and internal sources (FeedbackUI and VA Mobile). The model provides an out-of-the-box approach for NLP tasks, including NER, profanity detection, and stop word removal.
The data sources include:
Text reviews from the VA's mobile applications on the Google and Apple stores (external sources)
Reviews from FeedbackUI (internal source, available in the OIA_MobileHealth database)
Reviews from VA Mobile (internal source, available via CSV files on the Mobile VA's internal website)
Users: The users of this system are likely the OCC Data Science Team, who are responsible for developing and maintaining the pipeline.
Target Audience: The target audience mobile application developers and other internal stakeholders who may be interested in analyzing and understanding the sentiment and feedback from these reviews.
Problem to be solved: Consolidating feedback provided by patients and providers for VA Mobile Apps. Simple aggregation of data that provides a dashboard for clients to view and monitor trends.

items.name: Verathon BladderScan Prime PLUS System
items.description: Accelerate bladder measurements.

items.name: App Feedback categorization model
items.description: Consolidating feedback provided by patients and providers for VA Mobile Apps. Simple aggregation of data that provides a dashboard for clients to view and monitor trends.

items.name: LINQ II Insertable Cardiac Monitor
items.description: The device records cardiac information in response to automatically detected arrhythmias and patient-initiated activation or markings. The device is designed to automatically record the occurrence of an episode of arrhythmia in a patient.

items.name: VA Supply Chain Knowledge Management Dashboard
items.description: Identify indictments of the VA’s enterprise supply chain in OIG, GAO and other public reports

items.name: Human-Centered Design (HCD) User Feedback Summary, Analysis, and Design
items.description: The Human-Centered Design (HCD) team uses Large Language Models (LLMs) and custom agents to enhance user experience (UX) research. The AI system streamlines tasks such as analyzing user feedback, generating user stories, summarizing workshop outcomes, and refining user personas. The primary purpose is to improve the accessibility and clarity of user feedback, providing a better understanding of the needs of veterans and healthcare providers. The main problem to be solved is to maximize insights with massive amounts of app feedback to drive change.

items.name: Vivid E80/ Vivid E90/ Vivid E95
items.description:
Vivid E80 / Vivid E90 / Vivid E95 is a Track 3, diagnostic ultrasound system for use by 
qualified and trained healthcare professionals. It is primarily intended for cardiac imaging 
and analysis but also includes vascular and general radiology applications. It is a full featured 
diagnostic ultrasound system that provides digital acquisition, processing, analysis and display 
capabilities.

items.name: Avicenna CINA
items.description: The device is intended to assist hospital networks and trained radiologists in workflow triage by flagging and communicating suspected positive findings of (1) head CT images for Intracranial Hemorrhage (ICH) and (2) CT angiographies of the head for large vessel occlusion (LVO).

items.name: CLARUS
items.description: The CLARUS 700 angiography imaging aids in the visualization of the vascular structures of the retina and the choroid.

items.name: BTSSS 3542 Review
items.description: Power Automate AI model that was trained on ZZpatients filled out on VA form 3542, and scans the PDF's to rename them for travel staff. Decrease the time that FTE was spent on renaming files and creating a dashboard to search and sort the files filled out by patients.

items.name: Dragon Medical One
items.description: Solution to decrease the provider administrative burdens of typing medical notes.

items.name: Deep-Learning approaches to develop candidate lists of terms for use in text searches
items.description: The intended use is to increase the comprehensiveness of term lists to be used in text-matching approaches to identifying concepts of interest in free-text medical record notes. Text-matching approaches are an efficient method for identifying text of interest in medical notes.  The success of text-matching approaches depends on the quality of term lists used to search for a concept of interest.  While humans can generate word lists for text-matching approaches, the generated word lists are likely to be limited and biased by the vocabulary, language habits, population exposure, and local dialects of the human generating the list.  Deep learning methods can help to augment candidate term lists to help overcome some of these challenges.  Once generated these augmented candidate terms are reviewed by subject matter experts for appropriateness and accuracy of the suggested terms for the concept and use case of interest.

items.name: Identify access to genetic testing in Veterans with breast cancer.
items.description: Try to identify which Veteran with breast cancer has or does not have access to germline genetic testing from unstructured medical notes.

items.name: Cardiac CT Function Software Application
items.description: It is designed to support physicians in the visualization, evaluation, and analysis of the heart function through the calculation of parameters, such as volume and mass. The device is intended to be used as an aid to the existing standard of care and does not replace existing software applications that physicians use.

items.name: Purchase Order Filing
items.description: Filing purchase orders and documents related to them is time consuming for pharmacy staff and the paper files take up floor space.

items.name: VA CART Myocardial Ischemia NLP
items.description: Extracts myocardial ischemia information in VistA using Natural Language Processing (NLP) of Radiology report.

items.name: VA CART Ejection Fraction NLP
items.description: The model is available to facilitate risk stratification at the point of care. It extracts ejection fraction (EF) information in VistA using Natural Language Processing (NLP) of Echocardiogram TIU report text.

items.name: Limited Use of Azure Speech Services in PETALS Platform
items.description: Azure speech services allow the Interactive Voice Response (IVR) system to detect if the IVR should leave a voicemail or continue to a voice prompt to engage the end user.

items.name: Hospital Acquired Infections
items.description:
Automate the identification of hospital acquired infections to meet VA reporting requirements to reduce the reporting burden on health care provider staff.

We are creating a generative AI pipeline that will read through structured and unstructured data to identify the patients with central lines who got an infection during an in-patient stay at a VA facility.  There is no fine-tuning with this system. The patients identified are graded by humans and compared to known cases to assess accuracy.

items.name: Use of Large Language Models to improve classification of identified text snippets from the CLEVER natural language processing pipeline
items.description: Use of text matching approaches like CLEVER to identify clinical information of interest in free-text clinical notes is an efficient approach but can be over-inclusive (e.g. including terms when used with an alternative meaning, for example, a search for “arms” may pick up mentions of forearms and firearms).  A variety of approaches can be used to further filter identified text snippets to those more likely to be of interest for a given use case.  Here we use large language model queries to classify text snippets as being relevant to a concept of interest.

items.name: Executive Spend Analysis
items.description: AI is used to enrich Integrated Funds Control, Accounting, and Procurement (IFCAP) purchase order data by categorizing line item purchases into pre-defined spend categories that are more analytically useful. It offers insights into spending patterns and helps executives monitor and manage procurement expenditures effectively.

items.name: Scriptpro Ticket Report
items.description: Tabulating the scriptpro report is time consuming. It is a report they use to track pharmacy traffic volume and it was previously tabulated manually. They now use AI to automate for efficiency.

items.name: HTM112 Tutor
items.description: While current VA and Healthcare Technology Management (HTM) courses offer tailored content, the HTM112 Tutor elevates the learning experience by providing individualized and personal support that adapts to each learner's unique pace, style and specific knowledge gaps. It serves as a learning enhancement tool, not a replacement for instruction, by increasing accessibility to information and fostering an individualized learning journey. This ultimately boosts course efficiency and overall training effectiveness.

items.name: ValidateNow
items.description:
AI is used to help inventory managers and purchasing agents locate the right products from the 
Medical/Surgical Prime Vendor (MSPV) catalog that can be substituted for recent Government Purchase Card (GPC) purchases. It enables efficient management of expenses and ensures purchase correctness by surfacing untriaged items and comparing them to MSPV contracted items.

items.name: Document Detective
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: Built upon SSC’s automated tool, AAFC’s Document Detective Tool is a smart system that helps the Access to Information and Privacy (ATIP) team streamline and accelerate the process of finding identical or very similar documents. The tool’s scalable and flexible architecture ensures that it can grow with our needs and can be used in different areas without needing major changes for process optimization and efficiency. Discussions are underway to scale the tool in other departments.

items.name: Milo
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: Milo is an internal, enterprise IT chatbot first launched in 2019 that helps users get the technical help they need. It interoperates with the IT ticketing system to streamline IT requests and provides users with answers to frequently asked IT questions. At the beginning of 2024, AAFC started implementing improvements to the chatbot, leveraging the power of AI to further streamline processes, enabling users to get the help they need in an even more natural and conversational manner.

items.name: Extraction of Protein Digestibility Data
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: The project aims to use AI and automation to extract and interpret nutrition data from scientific literature, focusing on amino acid composition. It would then calculate amino acid scores based on various reference patterns and present the results in summary tables or graphs highlighting the limiting amino acid.

items.name: Arthropod Detection and Classification
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: Computer models are used to find and identify different insect species from images taken from sticky traps. The results are shown in an easy-to-use online dashboard to help track biodiversity.

items.name: Powdery Mildew Detection
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: The project aims to address the challenge of accurate and efficient identification of plant diseases, specifically focusing on the detection of powdery mildew. Powdery mildew is a common fungal disease that affects a wide range of plant species, leading to decreased crop quality and yield. The project seeks to develop a user-friendly web application embeded with AI functionalities that empowers farmers, gardeners, and agricultural enthusiasts to detect powdery mildew and make informed decisions for disease management.

items.name: ProteBee - AI Analysis Tool for Evaluation
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description:
This project aims to implement a generative AI assistant to support qualitative data analysis. This tool will allow staff to query and summarize content from SharePoint folders using prompts informed by evaluation frameworks. It will generate outputs with precise references, helping evaluators identify insights and refine their analysis.
Operating in a secure, sandboxed environment, the assistant may also reference publicly available AAFC information. By streamlining tasks like summarization and coding, it will free up time for deeper research and stakeholder engagement. The goal is to enhance, not replace, evaluator capacity, enabling more efficient, inclusive, and insightful evaluations.

items.name: Digitizing Insect Labels
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description:
This project aims to automate the transcription of insect collection labels using Generative AI (GenAI) models. The insect collection includes approximately 100,000 specimens with photographed labels that have not yet been transcribed into the central database. 
The solution includes using GenAI models to extract text from these images, both printed and handwritten, and structure the extracted content into a predefined format suitable for direct integration with the existing insect database.

items.name: OneHealth Emergency Tool
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description:
The project is to develop a flexible and scalable system in order to search and compile publicly available One Health risk indicators. Use cases may span zoonotic diseases, antimicrobial resistance, transmissible animal diseases, and plant and animal diseases with significant health and economic impacts. 
The project will aim at collecting and filtering publicly available data sources. Another objective will be to store and export outputs in a structured, editable format. And lastly, developing a cumulative repository while avoiding duplication.

items.name: Data and Digital Requirements Intake Solution
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: The proposed initiative aims to streamline and reduce administrative overhead in the Data and Digital Requirements (DDR) processes by incorporating AI and/or automation technologies.  The processes and tool is used by the Project Management Office as a business intake for new IM/IT business application or solution to automate existing or new business processes, enhance exisiting applications by adding new functionality, upgrade in existing technology enviroment and engage SSC resourcing.

items.name: Automatic detection of guns and gun parts
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: Machine learning algorithm in development for postal X-rays to automatically detect guns and gun parts

items.name: Fuzzy Search (SSAName3)
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: Fuzzy Search (SSAName3) is a critical software tool used by CBSA to flag high risk travellers and goods. Utilized to conduct fuzzy name searching and matching across a number of CBSA’s lines of business solutions (Travellers, Commercial and Intelligence & Enforcement), particularly mission critical systems. The software processes data across multiple different datasets so they are properly indexed, identified, scored and ranked.

items.name: Client Reporting and Engagement System (CRES)/ReportIn
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: The Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) developed a voluntary Remote Reporting tool to enable individuals subject to reporting conditions are able to report remotely to the CBSA. The app uses automated facial recognition software and geographic data to verify the identity and location of individuals. CBSA staff review every report made by an individual.

items.name: Primary Inspection Kiosk
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: Self-service option for travellers arriving at Canadian airports to complete Primary Processing while crossing the border.

items.name: Security Screening Automation Project
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: The Security Screening Automation (SSA) Project is designed to expedite the review of security screening referrals. While the project seeks to automate many components of the triage function to support officers in their review of security screening referrals, the deployment of a Large Language Model is the only AI component being deployed within the project. Data, provided as part of the referral, is assessed against pre-defined areas of concern. These potential concerns are then identified to screening officers to support their review of the referral. The semantic capabilities of open-source large language models (LLM) are then used to create short narrative summaries to assist the screening officers. The LLM is asked to attempt and identify the existence of certain pre-determined areas of concerns.

items.name: Travellers Compliance Indicators
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: Travellers Compliance Indicators (TCI) is a series of algorithms to assist border officers in identifying likely compliant travellers. It is currently in development and will not be in production before 2026.

items.name: Risk scoring for Courier Low Value Shipments
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: Development of a data science solutions to assess the performance of probabilistic models for the risk scoring of packages in the Courier Low Value Shipments (CLVS) stream.

items.name: Triaging RADNET alarms
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: Machine learning algorithm developed in-house to assess radiation portal monitor alarms

items.name: Export Compliance Dashboard
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: Machine learning model developed in-house to assess the risk of outgoing export shipments.

items.name: TradooIT
items.full_agency: Canada Energy Regulator / La Régie de l’énergie du Canada
items.description: Neural Automated Translation software used by the Translation Group for enhanced accuracy and speed given complex regulatory context.

items.name: M365 CoPilot
items.full_agency: Canada Energy Regulator / La Régie de l’énergie du Canada
items.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot is an AI-powered assistant integrated into Microsoft 365 apps like Word, Excel, and Teams to help users create, analyze, and communicate more effectively. It leverages large language models and Microsoft Graph data to generate content, summarize information, automate tasks, and enhance productivity.

items.name: BERDI
items.full_agency: Canada Energy Regulator / La Régie de l’énergie du Canada
items.description: BERDI (Biophysical, Socio-Economic, and Regional Data and Information) is an AI-enabled system that improves access to data from Environmental and Socio-economic Assessments (ESAs) submitted to the Canada Energy Regulator since 2003. It enables targeted keyword searches and exploratory browsing of data related to potential impacts of pipeline projects on the environment and on the health and well-being of Canadians. It uses machine learning to extract structured data and metadata from PDF documents and applies safeguards to identify and exclude culturally sensitive Indigenous Knowledge from the dataset. It is available online at www.cer-rec-gc.ca/berdi (English) and www.cer-rec.gc.ca/ciber (French) and through the Open Science Data Portal.

items.name: Develop a public facing Generative AI Chatbot for general enquiries leveraging ChatGPT LLM
items.full_agency: Canada Revenue Agency / Agence du revenu du Canada
items.description: There is an opportunity for the CRA to leverage technology in order to introduce new service channels, synchronized with our legacy channels, allowing Canadians to both start and end their journey in the digital space in a tiered approach, as expressed by external clients. The new channel will complement contact centers and alleviate the pressure faced by this service. Developed in part by CRA.

items.full_agency: Canada Revenue Agency / Agence du revenu du Canada
items.description: The amount of time to draft additional communications products on already approved and publicly available information is burdensome. Using generative AI tools could save a tremendous amount of time.

items.name: Genni

Genni provides a cloud-based enterprise-wide solution for employees to securely complete their Generative AI tasks for information up to and including Protected B, a functionality not currently available with public GenAI tools.

items.name: Digital Identity Validation
items.full_agency: Canada Revenue Agency / Agence du revenu du Canada
items.description: To access CRA's online services, taxpayers had to request a security code be delivered in the mail. The wait time could be 5-10 days, delaying access and increasing demand on contact centres. In March 2024, the CRA launched the Document Verification Service for taxpayers to gain immediate access by using their smartphone, an accepted government-issued ID, and facial recognition technology.

items.name: CAVIA (Collections and Verification Intelligent Assistant) Phase 2 - ACSES Diary Notes
items.full_agency: Canada Revenue Agency / Agence du revenu du Canada
items.description:
The objective of this project is to implement a self-serve product that enables efficient utilization and search of the diary notes in the ACSES system. A product could make this information more accessible to CVB users.
The solution should have the ability to search across all diary notes to identify specific and relevant information. This includes advanced natural language processing, capturing details that are commonly sought manually, such as previous actions taken on accounts (i.e. Requirement to Pay issued, phone calls made, associated account numbers, legal warnings), and other descriptive actions.
The goal is to improve productivity, accuracy and efficiency for employees who need to access and use the diary notes.

items.description: CRA employees used python to conduct text analysis (including natural language processing) for open-ended questions of four employee surveys. Analysis of patterns, cluster analysis, prescriptive, predictive models for a variety of programs and evidence based decisions.

Implementing GenAI coding assistants can streamline development workflows, reduce manual coding errors, and enforce consistent coding practices, ultimately enhancing overall productivity and code quality.

items.description: Implementing GenAI coding assistants can streamline development workflows, reduce manual coding errors, and enforce consistent coding practices, ultimately enhancing overall productivity and code quality.

items.name: Anomaly Detection: Detecting fraudulent transactions within CRA's secure portals
items.full_agency: Canada Revenue Agency / Agence du revenu du Canada
items.description: The CRA collects and stores vast data on users accessing its secure portals. This data is stored in the form of access logs and is comprised of billions of historical records that outline the interactions of all users with the portals. Given the sheer volume of data, we need to utilize Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) to effectively scan through these records and detect suspicious, and potentially fraudulent transactions.

items.name: APIDS (Automated Prohibited Item Detection) Algorithms Deployment
items.full_agency: Canadian Air Transport Security Authority / L’Administration canadienne de sûreté du transport aérien
items.description: Assessment and trial of different APIDS algorithms on multi-view and CT x-rays used at PBS. OEMs and third party companies are developing object recognition algorithms to work in conjunction with explosive detection algorithms to detect potential threats. Materials absorb x-rays at different rates based on their density and atomic number, creating a contrast image of luggage contents.  Explosive detection algorithm analyzes the densities/atomic number and size of items in a bag  and compares the results against a known list of explosives and threat masses prescribed by the regulators to identify items of concern. Algorithms are developed by the equipment suppliers and are updated every several years based on new regulation. Each algorithm is rigorously tested by TSA and ECAC regulators before certification using real explosives. CATSA works with Transport Canada to assess whether new algorithms need to be rolled out to CATSA’s fleet of equipment.

items.description: CATSA is exploring the possibility of implementing a Human Resources Management System (HRMS) to manage position changes, track historical data, and enhance capabilities for Pay Equity, succession management, and recruitment. items.name: Predictive Maintenance Algorithms

items.description: Using AI to help optimize availability of screening equipment by predicting when a machine might fail.

items.name: Darktrace
items.full_agency: Canadian Grain Commission / Commission canadienne des grains
items.description: Darktrace is a cybersecurity system that uses artificial intelligence (AI) to detect, investigate, and respond to cyber threats in real time. AI is used to learn normal patterns of behavior across networks, devices, and users, enabling the system to identify anomalies that may signal malicious activity. The business problem prompting AI use was the growing complexity, speed, and unpredictability of cyberattacks, which traditional rule-based security tools struggled to manage. The objective of the system is to provide autonomous threat detection and response, reducing reliance on manual intervention and minimizing the time to identify and contain threats.

items.name: Drafting Ministerial Correspondence
items.description: DCMF conducted an experiment to assess the potential of Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in drafting ministerial correspondence. The goal was to test if Generative AI could draft responses matching or exceeding human-written quality while being more efficient (time, completeness, error rate, etc.). The experiment was integrated into existing workflows with minimal disruption, adhering to Government of Canada policies and privacy standards.Future recommended actions include the implementation and integration of generative AI with existing systems to streamline workflows.

items.name: Analysis
items.description: Exploring AI to support both plain language and analysis of large quantities of qualitative information gathered in interviews, surveys and literature reviews to identify recurring themes and patterns. Evaluation of Copilot, ChatGPT and others for this purpose underway.

items.name: Grants and Contributions
items.description: Conducted an AI experiment aimed at revolutionizing how grants and contributions (G&Cs) are managed and delivered. Over ten weeks, we explored how Human-Centred AI and machine learning could be leveraged to enhance program discoverability and improve service delivery for clients seeking funding. To this end, we developed an enriched Large Language Model (LLM) based on the Department's program structure and program guidelines. As a result of the experiment, we have identified the potential to scale the approach, including as broadly as across the public sector. The departmental lead for the experiment, as well as the President and Chief Executive Officer of the firm that supported the experiment, will be delivering a live demonstration of the AI-powered discovery tool at the 2025 DPI Professional Development Week to showcase its capabilities in transforming G&Cs service delivery. Originally developed by Systemscope prior to their acquisition by Levio)

items.name: Chat GPT to support the evaluation of programs
items.description: Exploring AI to support both plain language and analysis of large quantities of qualitative information gathered in interviews, surveys and literature reviews to identify recurring themes and patterns. Conducting this pilot of ChatGPT Teams  as it appeared to have greater capabilities for evaluation while while respecting guidelines (particularly not using any personal, protected information).  Best practices and lessons learned being gathered to inform longer term solutions and standards with PCH function area.

items.name: PAT (Personal Assistant Trainer)
items.full_agency: Canadian Space Agency / Agence spatiale canadienne
items.description: PAT is intended to support operators’ space robotics training by creating a state-of-the-art learning experience that includes both theoretical knowledge and practical application. Familiarization with the Mobile Servicing System (MSS, aka Canadarm2) and its robotics work station (RWS) operational environment is the first lesson new recruits receive when taking the robotics training at CSA. Under this initiative, PAT will provide this familiarization training, first for the MSS currently on the International Space Station, and eventually for the Gateway External Robotics System (Canadarm3) located on the new Gateway Station orbiting the Moon. The goal is to provide a comprehensive learning experience for CSA personnel, ensuring they are well equipped to handle current and future space missions. This project aims to set a new benchmark for adaptive learning within the CSA by integrating AI to enhance operational efficiency and effectiveness.

items.name: Algorithms for fatigue detection
items.full_agency: Canadian Space Agency / Agence spatiale canadienne
items.description: Algorithms to monitor and detect fatigue and stress using biosensors.

items.name: Canadarm3 (space robotics) autonomy & safety AI modules
items.full_agency: Canadian Space Agency / Agence spatiale canadienne
items.description: AI modules developed for the trajectory planning, flight product definition, and safety monitoring in support of Canadarm3 operations.

items.name: Connected Care Medical Module - Clinical decision support system
items.full_agency: Canadian Space Agency / Agence spatiale canadienne
items.description: Artificial Intelligence Architecture for Spaceflight Medical Decision Support System.

items.name: Assess crop disease risk
items.full_agency: Canadian Space Agency / Agence spatiale canadienne
items.description: Development of a crop disease risk assessment web tool for the Canadian Prairies (Phase 1).

items.name: Manage maritime risk and manage vessels
items.full_agency: Canadian Space Agency / Agence spatiale canadienne
items.description: Artificial intelligence tool to improve maritime risks and manage vessels (Phase 1).

items.name: Automatize tasking
items.full_agency: Canadian Space Agency / Agence spatiale canadienne
items.description: Autonomous Tasking Optimization via Machine learning  (Phase 1).

items.name: Map and monitor shoals
items.full_agency: Canadian Space Agency / Agence spatiale canadienne
items.description: Satellite mapping and monitoring of shoals through Artificial Intelligence (Phase 1).

items.name: Provide predictions of wildfires using satellite imagery
items.full_agency: Canadian Space Agency / Agence spatiale canadienne
items.description: Wildfire predictions using satellite data and machine learning (Phase 1). Developed by the Canada Research and Development Corporation.

items.name: Microsoft Copilot
items.full_agency: Correctional Service of Canada / Service correctionnel du Canada
items.description: CSC has also begun testing a time-specific, limited roll-out (50 licenses) of Microsoft 365 Copilot. CSC is currently undertaking preparatory work, including the definition of cyber and IT security guidelines, to ensure the eventual safe, secure and responsible implememtation and adoption of this tool.

items.name: CorrCHAT
items.full_agency: Correctional Service of Canada / Service correctionnel du Canada
items.description: CSC has begun testing a solution that will help CSC employees to consult the Comissioner's directives in a more intuative format instead of searching manually via our current Intranet website. The core solution is leveraging part of NRC's AI Zone best practices and developed it's own version of the code.

items.name: Westlaw Edge with CoCounsel
items.full_agency: Department of Justice Canada / Ministère de la Justice Canada
items.description: Pilot project for evaluation.  Westlaw EdgeAdds an AI research assistant to Justice Canada's Westlaw Canada legal research subscription service.  Westlaw CoCounsel is an AI-powered legal assistant developed by Thomson Reuters that integrates generative AI with the content of Westlaw to expedite legal tasks.

items.name: CoCounsel Core
items.full_agency: Department of Justice Canada / Ministère de la Justice Canada
items.description: Pilot project for evaluation. CoCounsel Core adds additional features (drafting, summarization, document comparison) beyond what was available in Westlaw Edge with CoCounsel for Justice Canada's Westlaw legal research subscription service. Westlaw CoCounsel Core is a legal AI product developed by Thomson Reuters, designed to assist legal professionals with complex legal research and document review tasks. It’s part of the broader CoCounsel suite, which integrates generative AI capabilities into legal workflows.

items.name: Nuix Neo
items.full_agency: Department of Justice Canada / Ministère de la Justice Canada
items.description:
Pilot project for evaluation of Nuix Neo in supporting the digital investigation, litigation support, and e-discovery functions at Justice Canada. 

This initiative arises from the need to modernize legacy workflows, streamline evidence processing, and take advantage of new functionalities in Nuix Neo, such as enhanced automation, advanced analytics, and a centralized user interface.

items.name: Lexis+ AI
items.full_agency: Department of Justice Canada / Ministère de la Justice Canada
items.description: Pilot project for evaluation. Lexis+ adds an AI research assistant and other functionality (drafting, summarization) to Lexis+ legal research subscription service. Lexis+ is an integrated legal information platform by LexisNexis that combines legal research, practical guidance, and advanced AI capabilities, including a personalized AI assistant for drafting, summarization, and analysis on a collection of legal content, such as case law and legislation.

items.name: JUStranslate
items.full_agency: Department of Justice Canada / Ministère de la Justice Canada
items.description: JUSTranslate is a translation service approved for handling information up to and including Protected B, which typically covers information subject to solicitor-client privilege. Translations generated by JUSTranslate rely on cloud-based automated systems.

items.name: Otto
items.full_agency: Department of Justice Canada / Ministère de la Justice Canada
items.description: Otto is an AI platform developed specifically for the Department of Justice Canada to streamline and enhance the efficiency of legal professionals and government officials. By leveraging automation and generative AI, Otto assists users with tasks such as answering questions, summarizing and translating documents, and searching legislation, all within a secure environment suitable for sensitive data. Its role is to provide accurate, professional, and helpful responses, direct users to relevant sources for verification, and ultimately empower Justice Canada employees to work more effectively and make informed decisions.

items.name: Enhancing EquiVision by Using Artificial Intelligence
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: This project aims to identify potential employment barriers and foster the public accountability of employers for the implementation of employment equity in their workplaces. A user can explore data by employer, sector, and location on this website. This initiative aims to empower decision-makers, federally regulated employers, and business sectors by enhancing Equi’Vision (a Power BI dynamic website) with AI-driven solutions to better capture and analyze Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) data.​ Equi'Vision provides data on representation rates and pay gaps in private-sector workplaces. The project aims to empower decision-makers, federally regulated employers, and business sectors by enhancing Equi'Vision with AI-driven solutions to automate data collection, streamline analysis, and provides deeper insight into DEI metrics. Developed in part by ESDC.

items.name: ESDC Virtual Assistant (EVA) Chat
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: Empower employees with a Gen AI Chatbot that provides intelligent support for summarizing, editing, coding, and research tasks, while maintaining privacy and accessibility. Currently using the GPT 4.0 mini model and approved for Protected B Data. Developed in part by ESDC.

items.name: SmartTagging for HRSB/College@ESDC​
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: This project aims to identify metadata to organize 4 Million (with up to 20M total) documents in HRSB's information holdings​ using EVA Domain or MS Purview. Developed in part by ESDC.

items.name: Benefits Delivery Modernization (BDM) OAS AI AssistMe
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: Assist-Me is an AI-powered chatbot. It helps employees access and gather information quickly and easily. It’s a one-stop shop using the Cúram system, providing answers to questions in real time and boosting the efficiency of front-line workers. It provides staff with links to articles and generates responses based on knowledge management and training materials. ESDC agents using Curam in the OAS on BDM program will be enabled with an AI-assisted co-pilot that provides responses to agent's queries regarding OAS benefit policies, guidelines and generic eligibility criteria.

items.name: EI Research Companion​
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: The EI Research Companion is an AI-powered tool that enables business analysts to ask natural language questions about Employment Insurance (EI) Acts and Regulations, delivering fast, citation-backed responses to support requirement creation and pseudo logic generation for technical teams​.

items.name: EI Jurisprudence Search Aid Tool
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description:
AI powered tool to help Service Canada Benefit Officers  responsible for assessing Level 2 Adjudications, Requests for Reconsiderations, and appeals​
 finding legal references required to support their assessment decisions relating to EI claims.

items.name: Curam XML Assistant​
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: Enables developers to write CER XML code from an eligibility requirements document provided by a business analyst, while offering a chat interface to ask questions to understand Curam structure and codebase better.​

items.name: ESDC Virtual Assistant (EVA) Domain Assistant
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: EVA Domain Assistant is a GenAI tool that allows users to chat with a library of documents and limited external web sites selected and uploaded by the team.

items.name: EVA Smart Assist for Medical Adjudication
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: This initiative will contribute to the Canada Pension Plan Disability (CPPD) Action plan and workload strategy by organizing medical files to facilitate the review process and help reduce application inventory levels. This will significantly alleviate cumbersome administrative tasks, enabling medical adjudicators to focus on adjudication.​​ This initiative will enable medical adjudicators to use AI to more quickly and effectively locate key information needed for adjudication. The solution will use the AI COE- managed EVA Domain Assistant infrastructure which leverages Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Optical Character Recognition (OCR). This will increase productivity for medical adjudicators by producing an overview of the file contents, identifying key documents, through citation reference links.

items.name: Internal Chatbot Labour Early Resolution Officers Handling Public Enquiries
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: This chatbot will provide precise, real-time responses to client inquiries about the Canada Labour Code and related regulations, leveraging AI tools to navigate complex documents and deliver accurate information. ​

items.name: TFWP Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA) and Fraud Detection through AI
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: The TFWP LMIA Assessment and Fraud Detection through AI project aims to develop an RPA/AI-generated report that enhances Temporary Foreign Worker Program officers' efficiency in reviewing Labour Market Impact Assessments by automating data analysis and identifying potential fraud.

items.name: d@Media
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: This inititive helps employes draft internal media monitoring newsletters by using artificial intelligence to filter and summarize relevant news articles, based on predefined requirements provided in advance by the business users.

items.name: Employee File and Job Posting Triage
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: The role of this system is to help with the backlog management of new employer files. It's a rule-based system where all new employer files are split between two categories. The first category is made up of employer files whose risk level is not considered high enough to require manual validation from a Job Bank program officer. The second category is made up of employer files whose risk level is too high and requires a manual assessment by a program officer.

items.name: Record of Employment Comments Assessment
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: Block 18 of the Record of Employment (ROE) is a text field for information about exceptional circumstances not captured elsewhere. However, Service Canada often receives ROEs with redundant or irrelevant information in block 18. The Record of Employment Comments Classification (ROECC) model processes comments in Block 18 to determine whether they contain information about the Reason for Separation and compare it with the RFS in Block 16.

items.name: Electronic SIN Automation
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description:
Use of computer vision to help validate the authenticity of identity documents uploaded by clients, as well as, reading, comparing, and extracting data from them. 
These documents will be submitted online as scans and/or photos (e.g.*.jpeg, *.pdf, etc.).

items.name: LMIA Summary of Information
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description:
The Temporary Foreign Worker Program (TFWP) leverages an AI-generated report to gather data from multiple internal information sources. It analyses areas like the Business Number, address of employer, wage being offered, and checking for compliance issues or allegations. The outcome is a concise report that highlights essential details, enabling processing officers to quickly identify key factors and focus on key elements of the applications they are reviewing.
While this summary of information report assists officers, it does not eliminate the need for them to review and consider the various internal sources of information. Officers are required to examine all relevant information independently before making a decision.

items.name: Employment Insurance Machine Learning Workload
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: Recalculation of Employment Insurance (EI) takes place when circumstances change or new information emerges that could make benefit calculations inaccurate. Where the initial benefit rate or weeks of entitlement were underestimated and EI underpaid, the claim is adjusted to compensate for the financial shortfall. In cases of overpayment, the claim is reduced to recover the excess amount. When no changes are identified and the initial benefit rate and weeks of entitlement were accurate, the claim remains unchanged. The primary objective of the EI Machine Learning Workload is to reduce the time spent by officers on claim reviews by identifying cases where a recalculation will not result in any change. This approach allows officers to focus on more complex reviews that require intervention and precision to ensure clients receive the correct benefit rate and entitlement.

items.name: M365 Copilot
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: The Digital Services Branch of ECCC is exploring the Microsoft M365 CoPilot SaaS via a GC pilot program, to assess its potential value to the organization and overall scalability, identify challenges. This initaitive will inform decision-makers on whether to move forward with broader GenAI M365 integrated services once Shared Services Canada completes its RFP process.

items.name: AI for weather prediction
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: The Meteorological Services of Canada (MSC) and Science & Technology Branch of ECCC are actively exploring AI for weather prediction. In 2023, MSC and ECCC's Science and Technology Branch published an AI Roadmap that outlines the path for integrating AI into MSC's forecasting processes through to 2030. This roadmap will drive forward key initiatives, such as the exploration of energy-efficient AI weather models (e.g. GraphCast, ForeCastNet and AIFS) and the development of an all-Canadian model called PARADIS (Physically-inspired Advection, Reaction and Diffusion on the Sphere) a hybrid weather prediction system called "spectral nudging" that combines traditional forecasting methods with AI.

items.name: Custom course creation tools
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: Environment and Climate Change Canada's Digital Services Branch (ECCC-DSB) built custom course creation tools grounded in training material. Proof of Concept project to explore how generative AI could be used to create training material.

items.name: Virtual agents for service desk
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: Environment and Climate Change Canada's Digital Services Branch built virtual agents grounded in documented knowledge base for service desk. Proof of Concept project to explore the use of a virtual agent as a primary point fo contact for subject matter experts or for customers. Objective being to reduce the load of triaging service intake requests by providing responses to common questions using corporate knowledge bases, in a natural language.

items.name: Correspondence monitoring tool
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: Environment and Climate Change Canada's Digital Services Branch built a correspondence monitoring tool to monitor and categorize Form Letter emails. Proof of Concept project to explore the automated management of a general delivery mailbox. An AI workflow is scheduled to look at the gd mailbox on a set schedule and read every new email, tag it with relevant information, determine if it is an email of business value, and remove/report emails that are not. Undesirable emails (SPAM,Phishing,form letters,...) are archived and metadata is captured. There is also a reporting capability for the GD mailbox owner to view through a dashboard the landscape of emails received across time.

items.name: Internal chatbot
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: Environment and Climate Change Canada's Digital Services Branch developed our internal chatbot that can be used with Protected B information.

items.name: Meeting sound file transcriptions
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: Clients record meetings using physical devices that result in a .mp3 file. The objective of this proof of Concept is to extract the content of a .mp3 file into a transcription file with disctinct speakers, to generate a meeting summary from the source file, as well as to generate a translated version of the meeting transcription.

items.name: OCR/LLM CITES Permit Project
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: Collaboration between Enforcement Branch and Canadian Wildlife Service to apply AI LLM to create digital database records from scanned CITES permit pdf documents.

items.name: ISTOP-ML: Automated Oil Spill Detection
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: The Integrated Satellite Tracking of Pollution (ISTOP) Program, is managed by Canadian Ice Service in collaboration with Transport Canada (TC). Utilizing data from the RADARSAT Constellation Mission the program is developing an application of AI/ML to automatically detect and aid in the reporting of oil pollution in Canadian waters.

items.name: Water Quality Change Detection
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: Digital Service Branch-Emerging Technology and Experimentation Division applying AI to remote sensing data for the purpose of automatically detecting inland water quality degradation.

items.name: AI for climate prediction
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: CCCma is actively exploring applications of AI/ML to post-process seasonal-to-decadal climate predictions. Deterministic and generative AI models developed in-house have been used to improve forecast performance and better sample extreme events.

items.name: AI for climate prediction and projection
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: CCCma is exploring the implementation of climate emulators such as the Allen Institute's ACE2 Climate Emulator to enhance the ensemble size of climate predictions and projections to better quantify climate internal variability

items.name: AI for weather prediction
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: The Meteorological Services of Canada (MSC) and Science & Technology Branch of ECCC are actively exploring AI for weather prediction. In 2023, MSC and ECCC's Science and Technology Branch published an AI Roadmap that outlines the path for integrating AI into MSC's forecasting processes through to 2030. This roadmap will drive forward key initiatives, such as the exploration of energy-efficient AI weather models (e.g. GraphCast, ForeCastNet and AIFS), with aim of increasing efficiency and improving MSC services.

items.name: M365 Copilot
items.full_agency: Financial Consumer Agency of Canada / Agence de la consommation en matière financière du Canada
items.description: M365 Copilot has been deployed to all employees to improve efficiencies and generate value in AI-assisted hours.

items.name: Copilot in Azure
items.full_agency: Financial Consumer Agency of Canada / Agence de la consommation en matière financière du Canada
items.description: Copilot in Azure helps IT admins manage FCAC cloud resources, automate tasks, and optimize performance using natural language and AI-driven insights. It simplifies development, security, and operations across Azure services.

items.name: Media monitoring
items.full_agency: Financial Consumer Agency of Canada / Agence de la consommation en matière financière du Canada
items.description: Use the integrated AI tool to build more effective boolean searches for media monitoring.

items.name: AI Agents for Internal Use
items.full_agency: Financial Consumer Agency of Canada / Agence de la consommation en matière financière du Canada
items.description: AI Agents developed in-house in Copilot Studio for employee use, primarily for text analysis, consolidating large volumes of documentation, or eliminating manual metadata tagging on publications, speeches, etc.

items.name: AzureML for LLM models
items.full_agency: Financial Consumer Agency of Canada / Agence de la consommation en matière financière du Canada
items.description: Use LLM models for analysing complaints data, and develop a Market Conduct Risk Model for supporting a risk-based supervision strategy.

items.name: Automated Detection of Unidentified Fish Sounds in Passive Acoustic Data
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: This completed project used Random Forest and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) algorithms trained on manually identified fish sounds for detection in passive acoustic recordings. The result is FishSoundFinder, an open-source, user-friendly software tool.

items.name: News topic detection tool
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Identifying emerging topics that are relevant to DFO's mandate. Manually tracking and assessing the relevance of news content is time-consuming and prone to oversight, especially when trying to align it with existing internal knowledge.

Objective for AI: Use large language models and topic clustering to automatically analyze emerging news topics and determine their relevance to DFO. This approach will also surface what knowledge or resources DFO already possesses to address those topics, enabling faster, more informed responses and strategic planning.

items.name: Oceanographic Anomaly Detection
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: The goal of this project is to apply machine learning to detect anomalies in conductivity, temperature, depth (CTD) data, highlighting significant physical phenomena in the state of the ocean for closer investigation by scientists.

items.name: IM Chatbot
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: Chatbot to answer Information Management related questions, based on information available on the IM intranet site.

items.name: GitHub Copilot
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: GitHub Copilot is being piloted at DFO aimed at enhancing developer productivity and exploring the operational value of AI-assisted coding.

items.name: Remote Observation of Watercraft on Roads
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
The Aquatic Invasive Species Program is proposing to launch a pilot initiative aimed at addressing a critical knowledge gap in our understanding the spread of aquatic invasive species.  Invasive species can attach to boats, trailers, and related equipment—a process commonly referred to as the "stowaway pathway"—which enables their unintentional spread across ecosystems when watercraft are transported between water bodies. The AIS Program is seeking to understand watercraft movement into Canada via international land border crossings. Currently, the extent of this "stowaway" pathway remains largely unquantified.
To address this, the pilot will deploy remote camera systems at select border entry points to capture imagery of incoming vehicles. Leveraging machine learning, the system will automatically identify and flag images containing watercraft.

items.name: PSSIFence
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: DFO inspects hours of underwater camera footage to count and identify fish species through fence counts, which is a structure installed across a river or stream to monitor fish population. The amount of hours needed to review underwater footages will need to increase in order fulfil oblgiations as it is not sustainable.
Objective for AI: Replace manual analysis of fence videos to determine salmon counts by species.  Leveraging advanced computer vision technology, reducing the manual effort required, and providing more accurate and timely data.

items.name: PSSIScaleAging
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: DFO-Sclerochronlogy Lab is responsible for fish age analysis services to clients across British Columbia and the Yukon and is responsible for approximately 80,000 salmon scale aging assessments a year. Technicians require extensive experience and knowledge to provide scale aging assessment. With increasing assessments and limited resources, it will only result in limited analysis performed. 
Objective for AI: Automate and improve the process for lab technicians to analyze scale imprints from salmon to determine its age. By leveraging computer vision, particularly through deep learning models like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), the process becomes more efficient and accurate to assess salmon age.

items.name: Pacific RAG Chatbot
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Information detailing DFO policy and governance, science and research, and main activities in BC and the Yukon for management of fisheries are offered on the DFO Pacific Fisheries internet site. However, it may be difficult to obtain specific key information when searching the website for relevant information. 
Objective for AI:  Aligning on DFO's data strategy and provide information and data that is findable, accessible, interoperable and reuseable is priority, a custom AI chatbot  uses retrievable augmented generation (RAG) to retrieve information from the DFO Pacific Fisheries internet site.

items.name: PSSIOCR
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Many DFO programs store thousands of documents (scanned, paper-based, unstructured records) that contain valuable information for reporting, tracking, and decision making. Due to the volumn of documents, it is often unfeasable to transfer this information into a digital structured format. This has created significant limitations for the availability, timeliness and quality of data needed to make Pacific salmon management decisions. 
Objective for AI: To help modernize data workflows and ensure long-term preservation and usability of valuable historical data by automating the extraction of document content into a structured format through the use of OCR software.

items.name: Factoid Finder Tool
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Efficiently extracting and navigating information from machine-readable PDFs. Traditional keyword-based search methods often fall short in identifying contextually relevant content, making it difficult for users to locate specific information within large document libraries.
Objective for AI: The Factoid Finder addresses this issue by extracting text from PDFs to build searchable content libraries. It leverages Small Language Models to implement semantic search techniques, enabling users to retrieve contextually relevant passages from within the documents more effectively.

items.name: Optical Character Recognition Data transcription tool
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Science observations and research requires transcription and validation from hand-written field notes, including historical information.
Objective for AI: Digitize non-machine-readable (handwritten) documents and extracts characters into a digital format, to deliver productivity gains and improve availability of data from paper forms. Piloted with  ~100 year old salmon observations and present-day salmon stream inspection logs.

items.name: Data Validation Tool
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Digitizing handwritten or non-machine-readable documents using optical character recognition (OCR) often introduces transcription errors, which can compromise data quality and usability. These inaccuracies make it difficult to rely on the extracted information for analysis or integration into standardized systems.
Objective for AI: Leverage large language models to detect and correct OCR-induced errors by applying intelligent validation rules. The goal is to propose accurate corrections and normalize the extracted data into a consistent, standardized format, ensuring higher reliability and interoperability across systems.

items.name: AI-Assisted CTD Quality Control Tool
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Quality control of Conductivity, Temperature, Depth (CTD) data is a labor-intensive process that places a significant manual burden on oceanographers. This repetitive task diverts their time and expertise away from more valuable scientific analysis and decision-making activities.
Objective for AI: Implement an AI-powered tool that automatically flags CTD profile scans for deletion or preservation during the quality control process. This will support oceanographers in performing quality control more efficiently and accurately, enhancing productivity and allowing them to focus on tasks that require their specialized knowledge.

items.name: Characterizing Environmental Extremes Using Machine Learning
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: This project developed a machine learning-based method to identify environmental extremes by segmenting data into regions with consistent conditions (e.g., temperature, oxygen, acidity). Extremes were defined based on historical variability within each region, enabling more targeted and context-aware analysis of environmental changes.

items.name: Improving Streamflow Simulations with Neural Network Post-Processing
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: This project applied neural networks to post-process streamflow outputs from the WRF-Hydro model, improving alignment with observational data. The approach serves as a downstream calibration step, building synergy between traditional hydrologic calibration methods and modern AI techniques.

items.name: Labels Pilot
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: We are looking into using Labels back end for smart labels as well as a free copilot agent for the department to use in order to validate a user's classification.

items.name: Analyse development programming alignment
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: To understand what portion of Canada’s development programming in agriculture and food aligns with the Ceres2030 10 high-impact interventions to meet the United Nation Sustainable Development Goal 2 towards “zero hunger.” The aim of this work is to use machine learning to extract certain project characteristics, features and details at the output and activity level to enhance the details currently found in existing departmental reporting. This will help to elaborate causal relationships of outputs and activities that are buried within project documents and project outcomes presented in reports. This process will lead to building a platform for portfolio analysis and future predictive analysis.  This approach will inform the extent to which interventions and approaches, either individually and jointly, are in alignment with the Ceres2030 Interventions and meeting strategic, programmatic and policy needs and beyond.

items.name: Disinformation dashboard
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Using Watson Natural Language Understanding for named entity recognition to identify key organization, individuals and locations.  IBM Watson® Natural Language Understanding uses deep learning to extract meaning and metadata from unstructured text data.

items.name: Financial risk monitoring
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
