items.description: Leverage AI and ML techniques to to provide regular, proactive risk monitoring on Global Affairs Canada's financial data with an objective to reduce the level of financial risk in mission transactions.  Vendor's off-the-shelf internal audit tool customized with Global Affairs Canada specific AIalgorithm development for financial monitoring.

items.name: Data entry for grants and contributions
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: AI-assisted data entry for Grants and Contributions to improve data accuracy and consistency across several critical data fields. This is expected to lead to better investment planning, prioritizing, accountability, monitoring and reporting of Canada's contribution to international assistance.  Analysis of project documentation and data. Data preparation and identification of most pertinent project documentation. Implementation of various AI natural language processing models to analyse project documentation and data coding. Optimization and fine tuning the model to assess and attain accuracy. AI models to generate project coding and robotic process automation (upcoming) to assist in the automation of data entry. Tools and processes for monitoring and maintenance of the tools.

items.name: Export Control On-Line System (EXCOL)
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: EIPA permits are automatically granted by the NEICS & NEXCOL systems if specific conditions are met, through rules-based techniques (e.g., available quotas for importers/exporters). EXCOL is an interactive and computerized application that allows clients to submit export applications and certificates on-line using Secure Channel.

items.name: Enhanced Reporting Capabilities for International Assistance Grants and Contributions
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Global Affairs Canada is responsible for managing most of Canada's international assistance funding, and its staff are often required to assess alignment between project funding and policy objectives. Assessing projects against evolving and emerging policy markers is time consuming, and GAC looked for risk-informed methods to automate these assessments. Using manual assessments as a gold standard, GAC tested approaches to understand the trade-off between accuracy, transparency, and cost. It implementd methods using generative AI to encode the assessment frameworks and the domain knowledge of experienced evaluators. This approach assigns policy markers based on project summaries generated during GAC’s existing processes. Multiple policy markers have been implemented within a web-based tool which is currently in validation. The tool has an accuracy comparable to expert assessors and produces an accompanying explanation to allow expert oversight.

items.name: Document Analyzer
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Document Analyzer streamlines the evaluation process by automating the structured analysis of multiple documents, enabling faster review and synthesis of findings. It supports standardized assessments, reduces manual effort, and helps evaluators identify trends, risks, and lessons learned more efficiently. The tool also facilitates the adoption of generative AI within evaluation teams, improving both the speed and quality of evaluation outputs for program and project assessments.

items.name: GAC Translate
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Provides the ability for users to upload and get translations of documents up to protected B data, from most world languages. The use case was driven by the need to quickly translate documents with unknown content that might contain personal information, to be able to determine if they require immediate and official translation. Originally built for translation of Protected B Information for the Canadian Ombudsperson for Responsible Enterprise. Since expanded to all GAC users.

items.name: Situational Awareness
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Situational Awareness is a custom application built by the National Research Council, which allows Global Affairs Canada to proactively monitor information originating from traditional news and social media sources.  The platform uses Support Vector Machine libraries to score the news articles as they are ingested.

items.name: Document Cracker
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: To manage the wealth of information, a document search and analysis tool called Document Cracker was developed to help officials quickly find the information they need. Document Cracker enables users to search across a large volume of documents using a sophisticated indexing platform and automatically monitor new documents for specific topics, emerging trends, and mentions of key people, locations, and organizations. These features allow officials to quickly identify pressing issues, formulate a preferred stance, and track the evolving stances of other countries.

items.name: AI-generated briefing notes
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: AI-generated briefing notes using internal and external data. (experimentation phase) Experimenting with OpenAI cognitive services for AI-generated briefing notes. This is an attempt to generate a first cut/draft templated briefing for policy analysts on which to review, validate and augment to create a final document. This area is also of interest to Strategic Planning, Policy and Operations who has supported Global Affairs Canada's Data Science team's experimentation.

items.name: SWS-QA-Tool: Use of algorithms, regressions and data science models to assist in data verification processes and analytics for CFO-STATS
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Use of algorithms, regressions and data science models, and automation to assist in data verification processes and analytics.   This AI tool will replace an SAP tool that is no longer supported.  It will assist the CFO's Statistical and Reporting Unit (SWS) to improve it's Back-end QA process, cathing errors more quickly and reliably, leading to better data.  It will also serve  help to evaluate progress on the "Data entry for grants and contributions" project.

items.name: AI process tool - email generator
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: AI Process Assistance Tool is designed to assist the International Assistance Operations. It ingests and analyzes documents related to International Assistance programming, and provides recommended responses to common inquiries. The tool features document ingestion and preprocessing, natural language analysis, and a response recommendation engine. The tool is scalable.

items.name: Copilot Studio
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Supporting a citizen led development, Copilot Studio provides a platform for users to develop their own chatbots and agents, using knowledge bases and data of their choosing. Users can deploy the aganets and chatbots to Sharepoint, MSTeams, and as standalone or integrated apps.

items.name: Enterprise Self Serve AI
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: The 'Chatbots for All' initiative at GAC is designed to enable users across various departments to create and deploy no-code or low-code chatbots. These chatbots will interact with departmental data, files, URLs, SharePoint, and the intranet, thereby improving accessibility and efficiency within the organization. The enterprise AI self-serve platform allows GAC staff to leverage a variety of AI models, tools and capabilities across GAC's enterprise data systems. This platform will also allow staff to prototype workflows and experiment with new technology as it is added to the platform.

items.name: Senior Management Briefs
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Automates the monitoring and summarization of media coverage. By applying generative AI, the system scans and interprets a wide range of news sources, extracting key developments and producing concise, actionable summaries  for senior management.

items.name: Missions AI-Generated Briefs
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: This tool streamlines the monthly synthesis of Trade-related events and activities reported by Canadian missions across the United States. It transforms these reports into concise, regionally structured summaries that enable staff to spend more time acting on insights rather than preparing them.

items.name: GAC GPT
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: GAC GPT is a secure, internal AI platform developed to emulate AI Chatbot functionality while protecting departmental data. It enables conversational access to curated internal documents, supports domain-specific knowledge, and enhances productivity through Retrieval-Augmented Generation and customizable prompts.

items.name: Financial Accounting Chatbot
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: The solution streamlines access to Global Affairs Canada’s financial accounting policies by deploying an AI-powered chatbot. This tool enables staff to quickly retrieve relevant information, automate routine queries, and improve compliance, efficiency, and autonomy in financial and procurement processes, ultimately reducing time spent searching documents and allowing experts to focus on complex tasks.

items.name: Trade Commissioner Service website chatbot (Digital Experience Platform - DXP)
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: As a strategic initiative to foster and promote trade, the Trade Commissioner Service (TCS) is deploying a refreshed website to optimize end-user experience for Canadian exporters. In addition to deploying the new website through the Digital Experience Platform project, a public-facing chatbot is targeted for release in the fall 2025 to further enhance the experience. Generative response generation using Microsoft Copilot Studio referencing the TCS website with citation support; semantic matching and trigger phrase refinement for FAQ discovery; keyword-based redirection to Other Government Departments ; and telemetry logging via Azure Application Insights to capture user messages, triggered topics, session activity, and engagement metrics. These capabilities are supported by a multi-environment deployment pipeline (Dev, QA, Staging, Prod), automated unit testing via Pytest, and performance testing using Locust, JMeter, and Azure Load Testing.

items.name: New Export Import Control System (New EICS)
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: EIPA permits are automatically granted by the NEICS & NEXCOL systems if specific conditions are met, through rules-based techniques (e.g., available quotas for importers/exporters).

items.name: Conducting literature reviews
items.description: Machine learning to improve efficiency of conducting scientific literature reviews including systematic reviews and scoping reviews.

items.name: Project Cyclops for product label compliance
items.description: Project Cyclops aims to develop an online web application that would enable inspectors to use a digital tool/camera to upload images to assess whether a product label is compliant with Health Canada’s regulations.

items.name:
Science-GPT

Leveraging AI (LLMs, RAG) to assist scientists with pesticide review (grey literature, published literature, scientific studies)
items.description: The volume and complexity of scientific data for chemical assessments is rapidly growing. At PMRA, this has resulted in a growing backlog of chemicals to assess. Scientists at PMRA have identified the potential for Large Language Models (LLMs) to expedite specific review tasks. Building and testing this tool with subject matter experts (SMEs) is key due to the potential for LLMs to generate hallucinations. Science-GPT is a Health Canada Solutions Fund project which seeks to explore, build, test, and validate LLM-enabled AI tools built with scientists to assist scientists with chemical review and build internal capacity on AI. This project is supported by Health Canada Digital Transformation Branch (DTB), Shared Services Canada Science Experimentation Zone (SSC), and The Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC).

items.name: Food Label AI for Monitoring the food Environment (FLAIME)
items.description: The Food Label AI for Monitoring the food Environment (FLAIME) project aims to collect publicly available data from the internet relating but not limited to food products, nutrition facts tables and list of ingredients. This data is held in a database and machine learning is used to classify, create data models, and generate automated reports.

items.name: Dark Matter
items.description: Dark Matter is a database system developed to store results from predicted machine learning models. At this moment, the database is storing all related information extracted from product monographs, including related data to Drug Product Database and natural health products. This database allows AI systems to infer the importance of document and find relationships between different types of text. It also allows users to fix possible errors in detection done by ML models.

items.name: Observatory
items.description: Observatory is a visualization tool that allows users to find which product monographs are used to treat cancer. Observatory also shows all the Drug Product Database details of any current product and its older versions.

items.name: Grants and Contributions Forecasting
items.full_agency: Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada / Logement, infrastructures et collectivités Canada
items.description: Our organization has implemented a new AI forecasting tool to enhance financial processes, focusing on data accuracy, efficiency, and forecasting for our Grants and Contributions.

items.name: Bridge Anomaly Detection Project
items.full_agency: Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada / Logement, infrastructures et collectivités Canada
items.description: Use of Machine Learning to detect sensor data anomalies in HICC's major bridges.

items.name: Results Stories LLM Project
items.full_agency: Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada / Logement, infrastructures et collectivités Canada
items.description: Use of a fine-tuned LLM to extract numerical data from textual content to improve results reporting.

items.name: Parliamentary Affairs Dashboard
items.full_agency: Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada / Logement, infrastructures et collectivités Canada
items.description: Using AI Classification techniques to scrape, identify and present Parliamentary committee meeting topics, questions, interventions and speakers in an easy to consume dashboard.

items.name: Adaptation of Canchat
items.full_agency: Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada / Logement, infrastructures et collectivités Canada
items.description: Adaptation of Canchat to HICC environment and departmental needs: particularly knowledge repositories, models and use cases.

items.name: Chatbot to guide potential clients
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: Using a Chatbot deployed on IRCC's Facebook Channel to provide functional guidance to IRCC clients looking to make an application. Leverage dialogue manager, natural language processing to ascertain client intent and provide relevant answers to questions.

items.name: Identify routine applications for faster processing
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: Using advanced analytics to identify routine and straightforward applications for faster processing within these programs: temporary resident visa (visitors), visitor records, and spouse and common-law partner in Canada applications.

items.name: Triaging client enquiries
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: IRCC is using AWS AI services (Textract and Sagemaker) to help triage clients enquiries to improve response time and client experience.

items.name: Automate the review of non-complex applications for Temporary Resident Visas and Work Permits made under the Canada-Ukraine Authorization for Emergency Travel (CUAET)
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project automates the processing of applications for some Temporary Resident Visas and Work Permits made under the Canada-Ukraine Authorization for Emergency Travel initiative (CUAET). The system automates positive eligibility determinations, recording of positive admissibility determinations, and approvals for some applications. The system does not refuse applications or recommend refusing applications, or make negative determinations. All refusals continue to be made by officers. Applications which are removed from the automation process due to the triggering of a rule may still be approved by officers. Automation will also be applied to administrative steps that do not require human intervention. The system never refuses or recommends refusing applications, and does not use artificial intelligence or advanced analytics.

items.name: Passport Automated Decision-Making in IRCC’s Global Case Management System (GCMS)
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline processing of passport applications. This project seeks to examine how any identified unintended outcomes may be mitigated while supporting passport application processing and particularly how applicants are triaged by the system to streamline identity, eligibility, and entitlement determinations.

items.name: Integrity trends analysis tool
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: An AI-powered solution designed to streamline verification activities by automating the identification and assessment of fraud risks in temporary resident applications. By integrating various data sources and analyzing trends, the tool provides comprehensive risk profiles and detects patterns indicative of fraud. Its intuitive user interface and detailed reporting capabilities enhance the efficiency, accuracy, and productivity of verification activities, enabling proactive measures against potential fraud trends. ITAT uses data elements found and recorded in IRCC’s Global Case  Management System (GCMS) and the Integrated Payment Revenue  Management System (IPRMS). The data consists primarily of information provided directly by clients through their TR applications and supporting  documents. ITAT also uses information that CBSA and other partners have  recorded in the GCMS. IRCC does not use external data sources such as social media.

items.name: Administrative Activities Support Tool
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: AAST is a processing aid to help staff complete administrative tasks for immigration applications, including data matching and bulk email creation. Biometric enrolments are completed by Visa Application Centres (VACs) and IRCC offices. The client is photographed and fingerprinted using Government of Canada software and hardware and basic biographic details are exported and entered into the Biometric Module in AAST. Immigration medical examinations are conducted by Panel Physicians who submit the information electronically to IRCC, which is then entered into the Medical Module in AAST.The Biometric and Medical modules of the AAST use index matching of lists of biographical information between the biometric enrolments and immgration medical examinations, and applications pending those assessments. This helps ensure that biometric enrollments and medical assessments are connected to immigration applications in a timely manner.

items.name: Advanced Analytics Triage of Visitor Record Applications
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for all visitor record applications to help IRCC decision-makers process applications more efficiently.

items.name: Advanced Analytics Triage of Overseas Temporary Resident Visa Applications
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for all overseas temporary resident visa applications.

items.name: Family Class Spouses and Partners Overseas Applications
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for both the Sponsor and Principal Applicant of overseas Spouse or Partner applications.

items.name: International Experience Canada Work Permit Eligibility Model
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for a subset of IEC WP applications in order to help IRCC decision-makers process applications more efficiently. The IEC WP model triages IEC WP applications by grouping files with similar characteristics based on the legislative, regulatory and contractual requirements for each subprogram and participating country. The model is designed to apply pre-defned triage criteria to triage and identify “light-touch” routine cases where the eligibility portion can receive an automated positive eligibility Assessment. For the Summer 2023 launch, automated eligibility assessments are based on pre-existing business rules for the IEC Working Holiday Program only.

items.name: Spouse or Common-Law Partner in Canada Advanced Analytics
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for both the Sponsor and Principal Applicant of Spouse or Common-law Partner in Canada applications.

items.name: Automation Tools to Help Process Privately Sponsored Refugee Applications
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the processing of privately sponsored refugee applications using automation technology to help IRCC officers process applications more efficiently.

items.name: Automated Triage and Positive Eligibility Determinations of In-Canada Work Permit Applications
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for post-graduate work permits (PGWP) and other in-Canada work permit applications in order to help decision makers process applications more efficiently.

items.name: FinDec
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: Screens Express Entry applications

items.name: AI-powered assessment of applications for study permits
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: To review study permit applications involving different nationalities, such as those from Saudi Arabia and India. It reviews documentation such as proof of identity,  financial sufficiency, and interviews.

items.name: Passport Program Facial Recognition System
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: The Passport Program uses facial recognition technology to support identity authentication, help detect fraud, and prevent passport and travel document issuance to ineligible applicants.

items.name: Impact Assessment Decision-Support Agent (IADSA)
items.full_agency: Impact Assessment Agency of Canada / Agence d'évaluation d'impact du Canada
items.description: The Impact Assessment Decision-Support Agent (IADSA) will leverage agentic artificial intelligence (AI) to reduce the time and effort required to complete Impact Assessment reviews while ensuring transparency, accountability, and compliance with Government of Canada requirements. Current review processes are resource-intensive, requiring analysts to examine large volumes of technical studies, geospatial data, and public submissions. IADSA will assist by summarizing information, modeling potential impacts, and providing structured recommendations for human review and approval. This will improve the efficiency and consistency of decision dashboards, strengthen compliance monitoring, and enhance engagement with groups, stakeholders, and the public - while ensuring final decisions remain human-led and accountable.

items.name: Pro B AI Chatbot and Agent Interface
items.full_agency: Indigenous Services Canada / Services aux Autochtones Canada
items.description: A foundational tool allowing for interaction with one or more AI models, similar to CANChat and Radia in allowing interaction, agents, knowledge bases and limited tools to enable the organization to implement some of the other Use Cases listed under the ISC and CIRNAC desired projects. Initial phases will attempt to have a similar set of standard data sources to Radia Pro solution.  Future iterations may allow for intergrations with MS Graph for email, calendar and SharePoint integrations (when the department is ready for this).  Eliminating the need for MS 365 Copilot licences just for this functionality.  Internal Service related data will be the focus in initial phases as more risk assessments and engagment will be required before moving to more sensitive data use with AI tools.

items.name: Public enquiries center digital assistant
items.full_agency: Indigenous Services Canada / Services aux Autochtones Canada
items.description: Assists ISC employees with real-time draft replies and translation based on publicly available information.

items.name: Web Publisher Accelerator
items.full_agency: Indigenous Services Canada / Services aux Autochtones Canada
items.description: Uses AI to summarize content and generate metadata, cutting content publishing time from days to minutes.

items.name: Policy Research Assistant
items.full_agency: Indigenous Services Canada / Services aux Autochtones Canada
items.description: Use of generative AI tools to query GoC policy and open data.

items.name: Code Generation
items.full_agency: Indigenous Services Canada / Services aux Autochtones Canada
items.description: Leveraging the use of Github Copilot to support code generation.

items.name: Speech Writer
items.full_agency: Indigenous Services Canada / Services aux Autochtones Canada
items.description: Use of generative AI to drive first draft of speeches.

items.name: Performance Advisor
items.full_agency: Indigenous Services Canada / Services aux Autochtones Canada
items.description: AI tool that builds performance frameowrk and data plans that are OCAP compliant.

items.name: Leveraging the artificial intelligence-enabled components of the Onscope Search System for the Canadian Intellectual Property Office to issue pre-assessment letters
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: Use of an algorithm for an automated analysis of the goods and services in new trademark applications to detect potential deficiencies/issues prior to examination and, in unclassed registered trademarks, to provide suggested acceptable Nice classes prior to renewal. Results are communicated to clients in the form of pre-assessment letters issued by the Canadian Intellectual Property Office with information on how to address these deficiencies/issues. The issuance of pre-assessment letters has increased examination efficiency, contributed to the depletion of the backlog of trademark applications awaiting examination, and reduced processing times while improving quality and consistency of examination.

items.name: Onscope
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: Artificial intelligence-enabled components of the system use natural language processing, optical character recognition, and image recognition to make comparisons between existing trademark applications and registrations and newly-filed ones to assist examiners with determining the registrability of newly-filed trademarks. Since 2001, this artificial intelligence-enabled feature has increased examination efficiency, contributed to the depletion of the backlog of trademark applications awaiting examination, and reduced processing times while improving quality and consistency of examination.

items.name: M365 Copilot
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot integrates generative artificial intelligence into Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and other Microsoft 365 applications. This pilot license explores how generative artificial intelligence can assist with drafting documents, creating presentations, analysing data, and managing email. The objective is to improve efficiency and support knowledge work. The department is trialing this tool on a limited basis.

items.name: Radia Generative Artificial Intelligence Platform (Proof-of-Concept)
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description:
Radia Proof-of-Concept is an experimentation platform developed by the Communications Research Centre to provide employees with advanced generative artificial intelligence tools and explore efficiency improvements. 
Launched in 2024, it enables interaction with LLMs and autonomous agents through application programming interfaces. Hosted in a departmental cloud environment, it can integrate with internal knowledge bases and systems. It supports summarisation, drafting, translation, code assistance, data analysis, and text or visual content generation. Users can also design automated workflows. The goal is to reduce repetitive tasks, improve productivity, and explore safe, responsible AI adoption in departmental operations. The application is restricted to unclassified information. 
Radia Proof-of-Concept uses a combination of open-source and proprietary components. The platform is extended with custom integrations and enhancements for intelligent agents, knowledge base connectivity, and departmental system integration. Provides access to various proprietary models and services through application programming interfaces.

items.name: RadiaPRO Generative Artificial Intelligence Platform (Pilot)
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description:
RadiaPRO is a planned enterprise platform by ISED to provide employees with advanced generative artificial intelligence tools and improve business process efficiency. Hosted in the department’s Protected B-certified cloud, it will support interaction with LLMs and agents hosted by Shared Services Canada. RadiaPRO will integrate with internal knowledge bases and, in future, systems like SharePoint and GCdocs. It will offer summarisation, drafting, translation, code assistance, data analysis, and content generation. Users can also design automated workflows. The goal is to reduce repetitive tasks, boost productivity, and explore safe, responsible AI adoption in departmental operations. The platform is planned to be certified for use with information up to Protected B.
RadiaPRO will use a combination of open-source and proprietary components. The platform is primarily built on the LibreChat open-source framework, extended with custom integrations and enhancements for intelligent agents, knowledge base connectivity, and departmental system integration. It will provide access to various proprietary models and services hosted by Share Services Canada or other secure services.

items.name: DesignVision
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: Artificial intelligence-enabled system uses computer vision, deep learning algorithm and image recognition software to search tens of millions of existing design applications and registrations world-wide against newly-filed designs in mere seconds instead of having people to visually inspect thousands of designs manually. Since 2022, the system has increased efficiency and quality of examination, while reducing processing times as well as the need for manual labour intensive visual inspection.

items.name: Clean Technology Data Strategy Administrative Data Collection
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: The Clean Growth Hub leads the administrative data pillar of the Clean Technology Data Strategy. Its goal is to track federal investments in clean technology to inform and support federal programs. Currently, data is collected annually from multiple programs, which is time-consuming and creates reporting burden. To address this, the Hub is using artificial intelligence, specifically a machine learning model, to analyse proactively disclosed grants and contributions, identify clean technology projects, and classify them by subsector. The model was built using free, open-source software and trained on historical data manually gathered in previous years. This approach aims to improve efficiency, reduce burden, and provide timely insights for policy and program development.

items.name: Business Benefits Finder Daily Web Crawler and Daily Web Scraper
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: The Daily Web Crawler and Daily Web Scraper are artificial intelligence-enabled tools designed to automate the discovery and extraction of information on government business-support programs at both federal and provincial levels for the Business Benefits Finder. The business problem was the manual, time-consuming process of identifying and updating thousands of program entries. These tools use machine learning and natural language processing to continuously scan official websites, extract relevant program details, and feed structured data into a system for officers to review before uploading to the Business Benefits Finder. The objective is to improve accuracy, timeliness, and comprehensiveness of program listings, reduce manual workload, and ensure Canadian businesses have up-to-date access to funding and support opportunities.

items.name: Recognized Technician Monitoring Program (Pilot)
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: Includes multiple modules, of which the recognized technician monitoring module is primary. Collects qualitative information about service providers as well as the individual technicians who work for them, then assigns risk scores based on the collected data. Risk scores are then supplied to a model to ascertain the amount of monitoring appropriate for each organization and technician. Other modules are attached to this program which (for example) supply recommended monitoring dates for technicians based on risk calculated from previous performance history.

items.name: Executive Correspondance Workflow Automation Tool
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: The Executive Correspondence Workflow Automation Tool is an artificial intelligence-enabled application developed by Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada to semi-automate the generation of bilingual synopses of ministerial emails. The business problem was the high operational cost and inefficiency of manually summarising and translating over 5,500 emails annually, which led to delays and inconsistent outputs. The tool uses large language models to generate summaries in both English and French, maintaining a consistent format and tone. It also supports categorisation and routing to the appropriate sector. Human review is retained to ensure quality and mitigate risks. The objective is to improve productivity, reduce manual workload, and enhance the accuracy and timeliness of executive correspondence processing.

items.name: ParlBrief
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: ParlBrief is an artificial intelligence-enabled application to automate the transcription and summarisation of bilingual parliamentary committee meetings. The business problem prompting its development was the requirement for parliamentary affairs units to deliver summaries to the Privy Council Office within three hours of meeting end, resulting in high overtime costs and staff burnout. Off-the-shelf tools failed to meet business requirements due to meeting length and bilingual content. ParlBrief uses a combination of proprietary and open-source language models to transcribe audio, translate content, generate summaries, and assess tone. The objective is to reduce manual workload, improve timeliness and accuracy of summaries, and enhance employee well-being across departments.

items.name: Debtor Detection Anomaly System
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: Uses artificial intelligence to identify anomalies in estate filings submitted to the Office of the Superintendent of Bankruptcy. It supports analysts in assessing the integrity of the insolvency system by complementing existing manual selection processes, random Debtor Compliance reviews, referrals from Licensed Insolvency Trustees, and public complaints. The system is not an automated decision-making tool and does not replace human judgment. It serves as an input to improve risk-based oversight and could be adapted for other anomaly detection use cases.

items.name: Ombot
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: Ombot is an artificial intelligence-enabled virtual assistant on Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada's intranet that provides confidential access to information about the Ombuds Office. It was developed to address employee discomfort and delays in accessing support services, especially in sensitive situations. The business problem was the lack of a private, user-friendly channel for employees to explore Ombuds services without fear of exposure. Ombot uses generative artificial intelligence to deliver conversational guidance, with safeguards and governance measures in place to ensure privacy, safety, and responsible use. The objective is to increase employee trust, reduce barriers to support, and enable scalable deployment across departments through a “bot-in-a-box” model.

items.name: Automated Information Extraction of Spectrum Licence Conditions
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: The system automatically extracts spectrum licence conditions from regulatory documents and presents them in a structured, machine-readable format. This approach addresses the business problem of manual review, which is time-consuming, error-prone, and limits timely access to critical information for clients in the Spectrum and Telecommunications Sector. The objective is to improve efficiency, accuracy, and accessibility by enabling automated spectrum analytics and by supporting evidence-based decision-making.

items.name: Detection and Labelling of Anomalies in Spectrum Licence Site Data
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: Use machine learning to detect and label errors or anomalies in the spectrum licence site data submitted by Wireless Service Providers. Manual anomaly detection is slow, inconsistent, and resource-intensive, creating risks for compliance and spectrum management. The objective is to automate anomaly identification, improve data quality, and enable timely, accurate insights for planning and regulatory oversight.

items.name: Artificial Intelligence-based Web Accessibility Review and Enhancement (AWARE) (Pilot)
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: Automate and optimize web publishing workflows for greater efficiency and consistency; generate Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada-compliant HTML5 content and enhance web accessibility standards.

items.name: Qualitative Analysis of Public Opinion (Pilot)
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: System used to analyse structured and semi-structured data from public opinion research responses. The system uses large language models to increase efficiency and reduce bias in qualitative analysis of large files, reducing the need for a second analyst. This addresses the challenge of resource-intensive manual analysis and supports timely, consistent insights for decision-making.

items.name: Competition Artificial Intelligence Secure System (COMPASS)
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: A secure, Protected B compliant generative artificial intelligence platform designed to support competition enforcement and promotion. It addresses the challenge of analysing large volumes of sensitive and confidential information efficiently, which previously required significant manual effort. By leveraging large language models, the platform enhances analytical capabilities, enabling faster identification of patterns, trends, and insights in complex investigations. It processes public and confidential input from consultations and market studies, builds searchable knowledge bases from sensitive case documents and legal texts, and monitors competition-related developments from digital sources. These capabilities support efficient investigations, legal analysis, and advocacy. The objective is to improve decision-making, increase operational efficiency, and maintain the highest standards of data security and confidentiality.

items.name: Intelligent System for Content Analysis of Names (ISCAN)
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: An internal tool developed by Corporations Canada to assist with post-filing reviews of Individuals with Significant Control filings. It uses machine learning to flag potentially inappropriate, non-genuine, or offensive names for human review. The objective is to improve operational efficiency and accuracy in maintaining data integrity while ensuring compliance with legal requirements. The system does not make administrative decisions; all flagged cases are reviewed by staff.

items.name: Cyber Threat Hunting
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: To accelerate the creation of threat hunting queries for detecting cyber threats within our environment, this system leverages generative artificial intelligence tools, such as Anthropic Claude and Copilot. These tools assist in developing Kusto Query Language queries that identify potential threats in security logs. The primary objective is to reduce the time and complexity involved in writing advanced queries for threat hunting, particularly when correlating data across multiple sources.

items.name: Entity and User Behavior Analytics
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: The Entity and User Behavior Analytics system uses artificial intelligence to monitor and analyse patterns of user and entity activity within the department’s information technology environment. The objective is to detect abnormal or suspicious behaviour that may indicate cybersecurity threats, insider risks, or compromised accounts. The system leverages machine learning and large language models to identify anomalies and present visual trends over time, enabling proactive threat detection and improved security posture.

items.name: Debt Questionnaire
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: Uses artificial intelligence to analyse historical bankruptcy and insolvency data and compare it with information provided by Canadians considering debt relief. The business problem was the lack of accessible, data-driven guidance for individuals facing financial distress. The system identifies patterns from previous insolvency filings to suggest what type of insolvency others with similar financial profiles have chosen. It is a decision-support tool, not an automated decision-maker, allowing users to consider guidance when making informed choices.

items.name: NUANS Business Name Submission Prediction and Scoring Filings Improvement Project
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: A planned system to predict the likelihood of business name registration requests being accepted across jurisdictions.

items.name: Canada Business App
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: The Canada Business App, launched in June 2019 on Apple App Store and Google Play, helps Canadian business owners access federal, provincial, and territorial services for starting and growing businesses. Artificial intelligence will soon be integrated to provide personalised service recommendations based on business profiles. The objective is to improve navigation of thousands of resources by matching users to relevant programs and services using curated, unclassified datasets.

items.name: BizPal
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: BizPaL is an online tool that helps businesses identify the permits and licences required from all levels of government, reducing the time and effort needed to understand compliance obligations. The database currently includes approximately 1,200 permits and licences. Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada is planning to integrate artificial intelligence, including machine learning and advanced data visualisation, to address the challenge of navigating complex and fragmented regulatory information across federal, provincial and territorial jurisdictions. The objective is to improve accuracy, efficiency and user experience by providing businesses with clearer, more tailored guidance on regulatory requirements. BizPaL also uses generative artificial intelligence to produce simplified summaries of permits, licences and legislative content, which are reviewed and edited by the BizPaL content team to ensure clarity and accuracy.

items.name: Weights and Measures Marketplace Monitoring Algorithm
items.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
items.full_agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada / Innovation, sciences et développement économique Canada
items.description: The algorithm supports Measurement Canada in determining where and when inspections should occur to monitor compliance with the Weights and Measures Act. It uses predictive analytics to identify high-risk areas and overdue devices, improving efficiency and fairness in enforcement.

items.name: AI textual analysis tools
items.full_agency: Library and Archives Canada / Bibliothèque et Archives Canada
items.description: Pilot project to categorize a sample of 25,000 digital archival files

items.name: Transkribus Transcription: Canadian Newspapers Index & Order-in-Council Registers (1925 to 1940)
items.full_agency: Library and Archives Canada / Bibliothèque et Archives Canada
items.description: Pilot transcription projects using Transkribus AI model to improve access to historical records: (1) Index of Canadian Newspapers and (2) Order-in-Council Registers (1925 to 1940). Both projects aim to digitize and transcribe records for improved access and digital storage. Developed by LAC in collaboration with READ-COOP (Transkribus, originating from EU-funded READ project, University of Innsbruck)

items.name: Enrich metadata descriptions
items.full_agency: Library and Archives Canada / Bibliothèque et Archives Canada
items.description: Proof of Concept with Oproma to generate metadata for a sample of 1000 digitized government publications.

items.name: Canada Gazette Immigration Records  AWS Textract Project
items.full_agency: Library and Archives Canada / Bibliothèque et Archives Canada
items.description: Use AWS Textract to extract and index immigration information from 11,231 pages of the Canada Gazette for genealogy research. Developed by LAC using AWS Textract with ElasticSearch indexing.

items.name: CANChat Generative AI Chatbot LLM (Shared Services Canada with LAC training data)
items.full_agency: Library and Archives Canada / Bibliothèque et Archives Canada
items.description: Generative AI chatbot for unclassified government work, trained on Canadian data (including 50 TB from LAC Government of Canada Web Archive) to enhance employee productivity, sovereignty, and digital independence. Shared Services Canada (CANChat initiative) with training data support from LAC.

items.name: RG10 Transcription (ICR/LLM with Transkribus)
items.full_agency: Library and Archives Canada / Bibliothèque et Archives Canada
items.description: Improve access to Department of Indian Affairs and Northern Development historical records (RG10) via transcription of ~6 million pages, relevant to Indigenous history. Developed by LAC and CRKN (Canadian Research Knowledge Network) in collaboration with READ-COOP (Transkribus, originating from EU-funded READ project, University of Innsbruck)

items.name: Automated Document Classification for Network Shared Drives (NSD) to GCdocs
items.full_agency: Library and Archives Canada / Bibliothèque et Archives Canada
items.description: Automatically classify ~10 million documents from the former LAC Network Shared Drives (NSD) and eliminate ROT (Redundant, Obsolete, Trivial) information. Developed via RFP process

items.name: Census 1931 Index (HTR with Ancestry and FamilySearch)
items.full_agency: Library and Archives Canada / Bibliothèque et Archives Canada
items.description: Create a full index of the 1931 Canadian Census (~10 million names) using handwriting recognition technology and AI for transcription. Developed by Ancestry (proprietary HTR/AI) in partnership with FamilySearch; LAC provided digitized preservation assets

items.name: Chatbot for Facebook Messenger
items.full_agency: National Capital Commission / Commission de la capitale nationale
items.description: A digital program used to simulate conversation with human users around frequently asked questions through Facebook Messenger.

items.name: Deepl Pro
items.full_agency: National Capital Commission / Commission de la capitale nationale
items.description: AI-powered translation system designed to support clear and accurate translation between both official languages.

items.name: AI-augmented market research infrastructure, analysis, and support
items.full_agency: National Capital Commission / Commission de la capitale nationale
items.description: Service contract to provide artificial intelligence augmented market research infrastructure, analysis, and support as part of a research program designed to test willingness to support the NCC.  To accurately assess positions and predict human behaviour through a series of statistical techniques against randomized, controlled samples of populations constructed through social media.

items.name: Information Retrieval Chatbot (Long Wizard)
items.description: A chatbot to quickly search through DND/CAF policy documents.

items.name: Code Conversion
items.description: Converting programming languages from old languages (e.g., COBOL) to modern languages (e.g., Java, Python, C#).

items.name: Declassification/Redaction Tool
items.description: An AI-based tool to identify and redact sensitive information from documents before release.

items.name: Predictive Expenditure Modelling Tool
items.description: A financial forecasting tool to help financial comptrollers forecast in-year budget expenditures.

items.name: Translation Engine
items.description: An AI-based document translator that preserves all formatting from the original file.

items.name: Automated Compliance and Regulatory Classification
items.description: AI classifies data per security and retention policies, reducing compliance risks.

items.name: Agence short VR film
items.full_agency: National Film Board / Office national du film
items.description: Interactive short VR film and app that uses heuristic and reinforced learning AI to create a unique and random user experience.

items.name: Conversational AI
items.full_agency: National Film Board / Office national du film
items.description: Programming work and AI learning instance, AI process instance software,  AI Transcription Instance Software.  We use and have used machine learning processes in all 3 projects since 2019. All uses are in projects where the user/machine interactions are based on AAPs (machine learning processes) to create a conversation with users in the context of an artistic project.

items.name: Categorizing tweets for display
items.full_agency: National Film Board / Office national du film
items.description: IBM Watson Tone Analyzer ongoing service for live website

items.name: Speech synthesis
items.full_agency: National Film Board / Office national du film
items.description: Amazon Polly ongoing service for live website

items.name: Style transfer
items.full_agency: National Film Board / Office national du film
items.description: Style transfer tests on images during the development phase of the project. Forms networks capable of transferring the styles of works of art into images

items.name: Image interpolation
items.full_agency: National Film Board / Office national du film
items.description: Image interpolation tests in the development phase of the project. The technology was not used during production. Interpolation of video frames aimed at synthesizing non-existent frames between the original frames.

items.name: Virtual semiconductor fabrication model
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: NRC in collaboration with McGill University has devised a digital solution to reduce the impact of fabrication imperfections on semiconductor component's optical performance. This approach is a machine learning-based model to predict fabrication outcomes of design layouts. Designers can conduct virtual trials on the model, ensure that clients'expectations are met and collect useful data before sending their designs to the manufacturer.

items.name: Monitoring ocean health and carbon sequestration
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: NRC's Ocean program and a research team from the University of Victoria have partnered  to monitor ocean health and its capacity for carbon sequestration using a novel suite of sensors, machine learning and a remotely operated vehicle (ROV). The project also includes a cybersecurity component to assess vulnerabilities in the ROV's sensor network to protect data privacy.

items.name: Monitoring nutritional intake in older adults
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: Tracking eating patterns helps detect possible dietary changes to improve nutrition and extend lifespan. This is particularly important for older adults looking to prevent or manage dementia, continue to live well and age in place. This new AI research tool was designed to provide higher accuracy, simplify the monitoring process and lighten the load of self-reporting.

items.name: Enhance Transportation Infrastructure Resiliency through Bridge Monitoring
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: Canada has more than 50,000 publicly owned bridges. Many are reaching the end of their lifespan and 11% are reported to be in poor or very poor condition. While the design life of a bridge is generally 50 to 75 years, they can safely last longer with good monitoring and timely maintenance, which requires a significant public investment. The National Research Council of Canada's AI for Logistics program is addressing these challenges together with experts from Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada, Esri Canada and the University of Manitoba. They are developing improved methods for life-long monitoring of infrastructure conditions and decision support for maintenance investment by studying a highway bridge in Manitoba.  This will enhance the potential for remote bridge monitoring and reduce physical and economic risks related to bridge failure.

items.name: AI planner for truck routes
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: The National Research Council of Canada (NRC), the University of Calgary, the City of Calgary and 2 of Canada’s largest transportation companies have worked together to develop new smart AI-driven tools for truck-route planning. By anticipating traffic patterns, the new software enables planners to choose the most effective routes, which is particularly helpful in extreme weather conditions.

items.name: AI for molecule discovery
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: Molecule discovery is at the root of new health and other scientific discoveries, but the process of identifying the molecules needed for a specific cause is often slow and very costly. Artificial intelligence (AI) offers a faster, less expensive way of sorting through the large amount of data and huge candidate space to determine which molecules are the best fit. Using AI, the NRC and collaborators from Mila, McGill, and SickKids are currently accelerating the process of discovering molecules that can be used in healthcare and clean energy applications, while reducing costs. The project also focuses on molecule identification for developing green materials for clean energy, and on environmental protection, such as innovative battery technology to power electric vehicles or better methods for carbon capture and storage.

items.name: Drone detection
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: As their numbers surge, uncrewed aerial systems (UASs), commonly known as drones, will pose more hazards than ever to privacy, security, lives, homes and infrastructure. Over the past 4 years, an R&D team of engineers and scientists from the NRC's Digital Technologies Research Centre and the DRDC have developed an innovative technological solution for passively spotting drones in cluttered settings. It incorporates AI to accurately detect, track and characterize drones on the basis of the signal generated by their rotating propellers rather than by using an image bank. This method generates very few false alarms and accurately detects low-flying drones that use topography to evade discovery.

items.name: Oxygen predictor in the water of North Atlantic estuary ecosystems
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: NRC scientists are using machine learning to predict dissolved oxygen levels in Atlantic estuaries, a key indicator of water quality. This AI-driven approach can forecast low-oxygen events days in advance, even in highly degraded sites, and outperforms traditional deep learning methods. By anticipating problems like eutrophication and harmful algal blooms, the method provides valuable insights for environmental managers, helping them protect marine biodiversity, support aquaculture, and maintain healthy coastal ecosystems.

items.name: YeastNet
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: Developed in collaboration with UOttawa. Accurate and efficient segmentation of live-cell images is critical in maximizing data extraction and knowledge generation from high-throughput biology experiments. YeastNet enables accurate automatic segmentation and tracking of yeast cells in biomedical applications. Prototype developed and published.

items.name: QuaLC
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: Developed in collaboration with Uottawa. Digital Shadow of Liquid Chromatography system for providing warning about experiment problems. Prototype developed and in preparation for publication and hosting on complimet.ca.

items.name: AI-based, live visual bridge inspection by drone system
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: The AI automatically detects surface defects, identify them and highlight them in an augmented reality that is provided to the inspectors. The objectives are to make bridge inspections safer, cheaper and faster by combining the benefits of drone technology and AI along with other advanced digital technologies. Currently at proof of concept.

items.name: Global Public Health Intelligence Network (GPHIN)
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: GPHIN's mission is to be an indispensable source of early warning for potential public health threats worldwide including chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear (CBRN). Because time is of the essence, the goal is to use leading-edge communications technology and automated processes on a real-time 24/7 basis complemented by human analysis to monitor media sources worldwide and provide organized, relevant information to users allowing them to respond to potential health threats in a timely manner.

items.name: Food Inspection Environment Scanning Canada (FIESCA)
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: A platform to surveil agricultural trade publications in countries from which Canada imports food in order to detect e.g. pesticides newly authorized in foreign countries so that Canada can preemptively increase residual testing at the border.

items.name: Global Situational Surveillance Environment (GLOSSE)
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: A platform to surveil local news media overseas to assist international Watch Offices with response to local crises, e.g. quickly determine the most suitable evacuation countries/routes for Canadians overseas when conflict occurs.

items.name: ExaCT
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: ExaCT is an automatic information extraction system  that assists medical researchers and clinicians with locating and extracting key characteristics of randomized controlled trials from full-text journal articles. Clinical trials are one of the most important sources of evidence for guiding evidence-based practice and the design of new trials. However, most of this information is available only in free text, which is labour intensive to process manually for systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and other evidence synthesis studies. ExaCT is designed to partially automate and speed up this process.

items.name: Parliamentary Machine Translation
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: The National Research Council Digital Technologies Research Centre, in collaboration with the Translation Bureau, has developed domain-specific neural machine translation systems for the use of House of Commons and Senate translators.

items.name: EveryVoice TTS
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: The National Research Council Digital Technologies Research Centre, in collaboration with the University of Edinburgh, Onkwawenna Kentyohkwa, Blue Quills University, and W̱SÁNEĆ School Board, has developed text-to-speech systems in three Indigenous languages: Kanyen'kéha (Mohawk), nēhiyawēwin (Plains Cree), and SENĆOŦEN.  It is used to add realistic pronunciation recordings to text learning materials like dictionaries and verb conjugation references.

items.name: Surrogate-assisted optimization of a U-shaped actuator MEMS design
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: The system is to design and optimize an electromagnetic actuator for the Low-Voltage Deformable Mirror, aimed at compensating disturbed optical wavefronts caused by atmospheric turbulence applicable in optical communications and astronomical observations. We developed an AI algorithm, Surrogate-Assisted Multi-Objective Optimization, to identify more accurate optimal designs even in cases where data from simulations and prototype fabrication are very limited. It demonstrates the feasibility of this AI-based approach for electromagnetic MEMS actuators and electrostatic and thermal MEMS device.

items.name: Hydrogen Leakage Monitor
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: Hydrogen leakage is a serious safety risk for operation of hydrogen refueling stations. The system is developed to monitor the hydrogen leakage in real-time on stations. We developed a holistic method for calculating the hydrogen loss based on streaming data of hydrogen produced and filled.

items.name: AI for novel perovskite discovery and synthesis
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: Perovskite are a class of materials important in several engineeering applications including clean energy technologies. AI can be used to help identify and synthesize novel perovskites with tagarted properties more rapidly and efficiently than traditional trial and error methods.  NRC has developed a pipeline for the discovery of novel perovskites and for the indentification of optimal parameters during ball milling synthesis. This was developed in collaboration with University of Ottawa as part of the AI for Design Challenge Program. Currently in development.

items.name: AI  for scientific discovery
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: AI is being developed to advance scientific discovery including novel drugs and battery materials.  For example AI for discovery of lithium cathodes for battery applications. For example AI for T-Cell protein editing that can be used for personalized cancer cell therapies.  These applications are being developed with collaborators as part of the AI for Design Challenge program.

items.name: AI-based sea-ice presence forecasting model
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: The system forecasts harbour ice up in the north.  Accurate and timely forecasts of sea ice conditions are crucial for safe shipping operations in the Canadian Arctic and other ice-infested waters. Sea-ice forecasts can be obtained through an optimal combination of sea-ice model output and observational data, in a manner similar to weather forecasting. However, these forecasting systems typically rely on accurate weather forecasting data to drive the sea-ice model, which is not available at high spatial resolution during the advance tactical planning stage. For this reason, seasonal sea ice forecasts are often obtained through statistical means. This Project builds on a prototype system that used artificial intelligence (AI) methods to provide state-of-the-art seasonal forecasts of sea ice conditions in the Canadian Arctic. Codeveloped with University of Waterloo.

items.name: AI-powered drone system for aerial object detection and collision avoidance
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: Improved AI capabilites for drones (UAV) to operate more safely in complex environments beyond visual line of sight (BVLOS).  This enables UAS platforms such as drones to safely perform inspection, monitoring and modeling of the state of critical infrastructure and superstructures in the BVLOS (beyond visual line of sight) mode. This involves a threat detection and collision avoidance system enabling BVLOS navigation of multiple drones to and around an infrastructure / superstructure target using AI-enabled computer vision and photogrammetry to enable the development of high-precision inspection, mapping and automatic condition assessments.

items.name: AI-enabled system for multi-modal data alignment/cross-calibration for driver assistance (prediction)
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: System enables driver movements (gaze) to be aligned with changes in the external environment to permit AI assistance to the driver to improve safety.  It is an AI-based driver intentionality predictor capable of augmenting and enhancing typical advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) by broadening their time horizon. In the long term, having models that predict driver intentionality and actions can provide additional information to an ADAS that can be used to alert the driver or take action to ensure the safety of the vehicle and driver or avoid likely accident scenarios. Co-developed with the University of Western Ontario.

items.name: AI for signal detection of emerging technologies
items.full_agency: National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada
items.description: Articles in scientific publications often contain hints about important emerging technologies, but signals that indicate the next big risk to national and international security are often subtle. The challenge is to detect these weak signals and assess their possible impacts on future security, then use that intelligence to reinforce defence. For the past decade, the NRC and DRDC have used scientometrics, the science of measuring and analyzing scholarly literature, to track, measure and identify technology trends in different scientific fields. The NRC, DRDC and international allies and partners have prototyped an ingenious AI solution that simplifies and improves both weak-signal detection and monitoring of emerging technologies. It also introduces new techniques for predicting the impacts of these technologies and detecting opportunities for innovation.

items.name: Canadian Geospatial Data Infrastructure Geospatial Services Web Harvester
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: Uses ML/AI to automatically identify standards-based geospatial web services (such as Web Map Service (WMS), Web Feature Service (WFS), Web Map Tile Service (WMTS) and Esri Representational State Transfer (REST) services) that provide access to geospatial data. The ML/AI identifies and filters services available for the Arctic and Canadian domain (.ca domain). Purpose of application is to help identify services that are part of the Canadian Geospatial Data Infrastructure (CGDI) and to understand how widely standards-based geospatial web services are being implemented. A similar AI system is also operated to identify the same web services, but only those that provide geospatial data for the Arctic contributing to the Arctic Spatial Data Infrastructure (ArcticSDI).

items.name: CanmetENERGY Devon AI Assistant
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: Interprets and gives an opinion on data provided by user in the form of documents and media that will help formulate a customized response

items.name: EXPLORE
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: Improving industry operations: Machine learning software tool (EXPLORE) for process modeling, prediction and optimisation to reduce energy use and GHG emissions.

items.name: Automated process fault diagnosis tool
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: An ensemble of machine learning algorithm and causality analysis approach is used to automate fault detection and diagnosis, a task that is tedious and costly for the process industry and buildings.

items.name: CCMEO GeoAI Factory and GeoAI Data Series
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: Harnessing the power of AI to deliver value in the form of location-based data automatically extracted over time and space, to better inform mapping of Canada’s land, water, and infrastructure.

items.name: Flood Susceptibility Index
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: Machine learning to create a national map showing how likely a given area is to be affected by flooding.

items.name: Historical Imagery Processing
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: AI used to process and align (i.e. apply onto a geo-referencing system) historical imagery.  This opens access to the images and enables study of changes over time. The system includes automatic alignment, super-resolution and colorization of NAPL imagery.

items.name: Automated LiDAR PointCloud Classification
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: Provide a framework to automatically classify buildings, ground, surface water and other features from LiDAR and LiDAR-derived products. The framework contains trained model, documentation, indications to improve models (if required) and knowledge transfer to the production teams.

items.name: Canadian Geospatial Foundation Model
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: Development of foundation geospatial model to the Canadian context. Creating a unified, inclusive and more comprehensive frameworks to increase efficiency, operationalization and scalability of all individual geospatial AI solutions.

items.name: Computationally-driven design of molecular gels for liquid and supercritical CO2 capture
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: Apply computational and machine learning methods to develop and design CO2-philic LMWGs by identifying potential building block molecules, constructing molecular models, designing simulation protocols, and conducting molecular simulations to guide and optimize discovery efforts.

items.name: Electric Arc Furnace Properties Time Series Forecasting Tool
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: Provide modelers and industry with stochastic electric arc furnace (EAF) gas composition, flow rate, temperature, and pressure.

items.name: AI tool for CFD modelling post-processing
items.full_agency: Natural Resources Canada / Ressources naturelles Canada
items.description: This study shows how artificial intelligence (AI) can make industrial decarbonization research faster, smarter, and more effective. Specifically, we applied AI to analyze simulations of electric arc furnaces (EAFs), where biomass-derived carbon particles are injected to reduce carbon emissions in steel production. Traditional simulation analysis relies heavily on human interpretation of complex data, which can miss hidden patterns.
